title: "Healthy Brain in a Toxic World: Focus, Memory Retention & More”
description: Learn how to train up a healthy generation in a world full of convenience food and harmful toxins. Discover God's good food that supports a healthy brain and the body's ability to eliminate harmful toxins. Plus, find out how aromatherapy supports optimal brain health, focus, memory retention, sensory integration and learning potential.

title: “Move & Groove to Grammar:  Fun, Kinesthetic Activities That Increase English Mastery”
description: Focuses on strategies and ideas for teaching English by incorporating fun, kinesthetic activities, a component of mastery learning. Through kinesthetics, learning is, actually, expedited. This means using motions, songs, etc., to embed and to expedite the learning of standard English. Attendees participate in active learning, memorable learning, and “do and say” learning that gives children the power to learn 90% faster. Includes discussion of the prepositional approach that makes understanding language concept “a breeze.”

title: “Nutrition 101: Food, Hormones, & Emotions… Are They Connected?”
description: Discover how to use nutrition and health curriculum “Nutrition 101: Choose Life!” for all grades and ages as a health or science credit. PLUS, learn how to use it as a valuable lifetime health resource to naturally navigate various family health concerns including hormone and emotional health for moms, dads, teens, pre-teens, and even littles. Sera also will share her PRO TIPS on how to help picky eaters love God's food.

title: #UseYourYouth
description:
Part 1- Hear the story of growth from a once lack-luster, screen junkie middle schooler to a passionate powerhouse and voice for the Lord; the origin of #UseYourYouth
Part 2- Gain the outside-looking-in perspective of a young Christian, conservative homeschool graduate regarding today’s younger generation and potential pitfalls in their world.
Part 3- Explore the benefits of using your youth for the Lord and learn applicable strategies to become a confident, formidable force in a increasingly more confused society.

title: 10 BIGGEST MISTAKES HOMESTEADERS MAKE
description: It’s not all rainbows and roses. The unfortunate truth is that many wanna-be homesteaders burn out in about 3 years due to specific decisions or expectations that derailed their dreams. You will have a much higher chance of success if you don’t do these 10 things.

title: 10 Tech Skills Every Homeschooler Should Know
5 Core Habits to Help Your Child Learn Anything
description:
Discover how you can help your child acquire the essential habits to learn anything. This talk
will model these "core habits," allowing you to experience them yourself!

title: 5 Steps to a Superior Science Education
description: Learn the 5 vital steps that will ensure you impart a superior science education that inspires a love for learning, builds a strong foundation in scientific thinking, and contributes to an everlasting faith in God and the creation model.

title: A Better Life through Grammar and Rhetoric
description: Is the study of grammar merely learning parts of speech and sentence diagramming? No! The disciplines of grammar are so much more: Naming, Attending, Memorizing, Expressing and Storytelling—skills which create a foundation for all thinking. Rhetoric—an old and stuffy liberal art useful only for writing persuasive essays? Think again! Andrew Pudewa will show how the tools of invention, arrangement and habit, repetition, even hyperbole and metaphor can help you to persuade not only others but even yourself! Expand your idea of what grammar and rhetoric can be and see how these practices will come alive in your life and in your teaching.

title: A Biblical History of American Money (and Why it Matters to You)
description: Do you know what was the first kind of money used in the Bible? Do you know what a "piece of eight" is, or where the expression "not worth a Continental" comes from? Come to this workshop and hold actual biblical money in your hand! Learn why the money we carry in our pockets today is neither biblical nor Constitutional, why Roger Sherman is an unknown but heroic founding father, and how all of this impacts your family today.

title: A Charlotte Mason Education – Excellence Without Sacrifice
description: Learn the foundational tenets of a Charlotte Mason education and how to implement her methodology in your homeschool.

title: A New Elementary-School Approach for Discovering Music and Art
AI in Your Homeschool
description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a tool that can be both used and abused by students. In this talk, Dr. Jay L. Wile discusses a few examples of AI that anyone can use. He goes over ways in which AI can legitimately help a student learn more about a subject as well as the ways in which a student can use AI in an illegitimate manner. He also covers tools that you can use to check how your student is using AI. Finally, he gives his view on whether or not AI will ever be able to mimic human intelligence.

title: AI is Here to Stay. How to Incorporate AI Into Your Homeschool
description: Artificial intelligence is here to stay whether or not we like it. Should you incorporate AI into your homeschool curricula? We’ll discuss the pros and cons of incorporating it into your curriculum and show you examples of ways to implement it if you decide it is right for your school.

title: AI or RI?: Should We Even Teach Math, Spelling, and Writing?
description: Technology will atrophy the skill it replaces. While this is not a new problem, it seems to now be a controversy on steroids. What tasks should we delegate to artificial intelligence and what should we retain as “real intelligence”—and why? Join Andrew Pudewa as we look at some basic things humans have taught and learned for years while contemplating some possible answers to this rapidly growing question: When and how should students begin using AI? Hacking High School…and College!?! (Revised from 2023, now with Nick Ellis) Most of us have presuppositions about what high school is based on our own experience. Times are changing, and the opportunities for home educating high schoolers have grown rapidly. Many of these opportunities will save time, help your family avoid higher education debt, and give your teens a head start on their next decade of life. Join Andrew Pudewa and Nick Ellis for a lively, challenging look at alternatives for motivated young adults.

title: All the Questions You're Afraid to Ask About Marriage
All the Questions You're Afraid to Ask About Marriage
America’s Miraculous Story: Evidence of God’s Hand
American Slavery: Truth vs. Fiction
description: The true history of American slavery can be hard to distinguish. This lecture explores the facts of this horrific time in American history, as well as its origins, position in a global context, the Founding Fathers' own views on the issue, and the process America took to end the wicked practice. How does this information affect the narrative that is being used today in regards to racism?

title: Anchoring on Your “Why” When Times get Tough: From Teaching Neurodivergent Students to Life’s Difficult Seasons
description: Homeschooling through difficult seasons can test even the most committed families. This workshop offers encouragement for parents teaching neurodivergent or special needs students, navigating busy schedules or family emergencies, and learning when to pause without quitting. Together we’ll refocus on your “why” and find renewed strength to continue the journey.

title: Anchoring on Your “Why” When Times get Tough: From Teaching Neurodivergent Students to Life’s Difficult Seasons
description: Homeschooling through difficult seasons can test even the most committed families. This workshop offers encouragement for parents teaching neurodivergent or special needs students, navigating busy schedules or family emergencies, and learning when to pause without quitting. Together we’ll refocus on your “why” and find renewed strength to continue the journey.

title: Art as a Superpower: Teaching Kids to See, Feel, and Create Deeply
description: What if art could teach empathy, patience, and purpose? Dwayne Walker explores how artistic practice develops not just skill, but character. You’ll learn how to guide your child beyond technique into creative expression that shapes who they become.

title: Ask the Beast
description: In this seminar, Dr. Wile talks about Job 12:7-10, which begins by commanding the reader to “Ask the beasts.” In his view, this Scripture is telling us that we must study nature to fully understand God. While God’s divine revelation (the Bible) is clearly more detailed and precise than His natural revelation (creation), both are necessary to have a full and complete understanding God. He gives a bit of history to show you that this was the motivation for many of the great scientists of the past, and some great scientists of the present. He then draws from fields such as astronomy, microbiology, zoology, and botany to share at least a bit of what science tells us about God.

title: Assessment without Grades
description: For a blink of history, government schools have experimented with institutional 'grading' and embedded it into our culture as 'essential' for learning. Yet, for thousands of years, educators have used other methods to evaluate learning that are more individualized and effective than testing and letter grades. Find out more about assessment options, fit, and appropriate use of a broader range of tools. Dr. Dikkers will provide context, examples, and cognitive science to support your move to 'project' and 'conversational' assessments so you can confidently recognize and document your child's learning.

title: Astronomy High School Curriculum
description:
The motivation that inspired a team of Christian professional astronomers to develop this textbook was to equip and prepare Christian students for the challenges they will face at secular universities. Statistics are clear in showing that many Christians lose their faith during their college years and certainly confusion about science and faith play an important role in this outcome. This unique curriculum was born out of a desire to make a difference in changing these statistics and to equip Christian students to defend their faith with sound reasoning in a secular environment. This one-year science curriculum consists of two parts: (1) Introduction to the science and faith dialogue and (2) Astronomy as a discipline including many interviews with top level professional Christian astronomers to engage and inspire students; (more information at www.GlimpseofHisSplendor.com).
In this workshop we plan to cover topics and a few exercises on the introduction to the science and faith dialogue, which includes:
• science and worldviews – how to discern between worldviews and scientific information
• definitions of the realm of science and faith, the applicability and limitations of the scientific method, the search for truth from both a scientific and religious standpoint;
• four views on the relationship between science and faith (conflict, independence, dialogue and integration)
• Exposing the Conflicts – scientists, Christians and human attitudes
• tips and tools for making sound arguments.
The workshop will also briefly discuss fingerprints of God in the universe. It will present evidence for a beginning and introduce the fine tuning in the laws of physics, which is widely regarded as the most persuasive current argument for the existence of a Super Intelligence, a Designer for this universe.

title: Banishing Math Anxiety: A Guide to Generational Success
description:
Math anxiety can feel like an insurmountable obstacle at any age, hindering progress and fostering a
negative mindset. But what if you could transform that apprehension into confidence? This workshop
helps you overcome challenges by assessing your math apprehension, addressing knowledge gaps, and
setting achievable goals. While beneficial for all ages, it's especially valuable for parents of struggling
middle and high school students, offering tools to support their children and build a positive, lasting
relationship with numbers.

title: Be Sure and Secure
description:
The "Be Sure and Secure" workshop is an interactive, family-focused session designed to equip homeschool parents and students with foundational cybersecurity skills, using the BE SURE framework: Backup, Encrypt, Secure, Update, Reduce Risk, and Educate. Participants learn how to protect digital devices, safeguard personal information, and develop healthy online habits using practical, easy-to-follow steps tailored for the home environment.
With live demonstrations, real-world examples, and actionable take-home strategies, this workshop empowers attendees to confidently address digital risks, teach safe technology practices, and create a safer online experience for the entire family. Perfect for tech beginners and seasoned parents alike, "Be Sure and Secure" offers a clear path to building lasting digital resilience in today’s connected world.

title: Becoming Bullyproof: Raising Teens Who Don’t Break
description:
Bullying is everywhere—at co-ops, on group chats, even in church youth groups. And while we can’t always prevent our teens from being targeted, we can teach them how to become unshakable in the face of it.

In this transformative workshop, Joey Mascio (former middle school teacher, professional performer, and creator of Sidekick to Hero) shares his 5 Rules for Becoming Bullyproof—an empowering framework that helps teens take control of their emotional world and stop seeing themselves as victims.

Instead of relying on vague advice like “just ignore it,” Joey equips parents with practical tools based in cognitive behavioral coaching, storytelling, and real teen psychology. You’ll learn how to help your teen:

• Stop giving power to mean words or people
• Own their flaws and love who they are
• See through the illusion of comparison
• Reframe “bullies” as people—not villains
• Know when and how to seek justice

Whether your teen is the target of teasing or just navigating the awkward jungle of adolescence, this session will help you guide them to develop real resilience, build true self-worth, and rise stronger—no matter what comes at them.

title: Becoming You: The Power of Public Speaking to Shape the Heart and Mind
description: Becoming You: The Power of Public Speaking to Shape the Heart and Mind is a transformative class designed for homeschool teens (and their supportive parents!) who want to grow in both confidence and character. Through engaging public speaking activities and meaningful self-reflection, students will explore how developing their authentic voice helps cultivate the "8 C’s" of inner strength: calm, clarity, compassion, confidence, connection, curiosity, courage, and creativity. This class offers a safe, encouraging space to speak from the heart, listen deeply, and grow into the person they’re meant to be—on stage and in life.

title: Behind the Scenes of Storytelling (designed for 5th-8th grade students)
description: How can you take pieces of your real life—or things from your imagination—and create a story? Award-winning middle grade author Becca Wierwille shares her story creation process with young readers and encourages them to create stories from their own lives and imaginations. This presentation includes details about Becca’s story creation process, tips for students who want to create their own stories, a group writing exercise, an opportunity for young readers to workshop with each other and practice creating their own stories, and Q&A time at the end.

title: Beyond ADHD Symptoms: Exploring Causes, Understanding Holistic Treatments, and Mastering Learning Strategies to Treat the Whole Child
description:
Have you always wanted to improve your child’s ability to follow multiple step
instructions, read more fluently, or grasp those tricky math word problems? Have you wondered if there are
things you can do as the teacher to improve your child’s progress? The field of neuroscience has given us an
immense amount of information on how the brain learns and provides a roadmap of how to teach
considering brain function to optimize learning. Take away easy-to-implement actionable steps that are
quick and affordable but can make a huge impact in your homeschool classroom.
Participants will learn:
~What neuroplasticity is
~The definition of the 7 core cognitive skills and how these impact learning and teaching
~How to identify cognitive weaknesses in your students
~Classroom activities to support cognitive development

title: Beyond the Lessons: Reaching Your Child’s Heart in Homeschooling
description: Homeschooling can sometimes feel like a whirlwind of lessons, laundry, and life—but beneath it all beats the heart of a child who needs to be known, loved, and guided toward God. Drawing on Proverbs 4:23 and two decades of homeschooling her 10 children, Cassandra DeLeon invites moms to slow down and refocus on the heart behind homeschooling. Learn how to move beyond checklists and cultivate deep, lasting connections that shape your child’s faith and character for a lifetime.

title: Biblical Reflections in Stories
description: Can you really grow your love of God’s Word by reading fiction? Even fantasy and science fiction? Christian Hauer, author of “The Bygone Sagas of Oogerel” and “The Marvelous Mr Motley”, brings his listeners into a discussion about exploring fiction that dives into biblical ethics and the building of the moral imagination in readers.

title: Bilingual Education for Homeschool Families: How to Start and Keep Your  Family’s Bilingual Legacy (English)
description: Teaching a second language at home can feel overwhelming, but it’s also one of the most rewarding and enriching gifts you can give your children. In this workshop, we’ll explore how to begin and sustain your family’s language legacy. You’ll learn about bilingual instructional models and discover how to create a rich bilingual environment where your children can thrive academically while staying connected to both languages and cultures. Whether you're just starting out or looking to strengthen your current approach, this session will equip you with practical tools to build and preserve a lasting legacy through language and heritage.

title: Blow Up Your Life: Taking the Next Best Step
description: Parents and students often feel pressure to map out an entire career before graduation. What if the goal isn’t having a 30-year plan—but knowing how God uniquely wired you? In this session, we’ll talk about discernment, calling, skill development, and practical next steps students can take right now to move forward with clarity and confidence.

title: Boys R Us: Connect with Your Son like Never Before
description:
This workshop draws on Dr. Currie’s expertise as a psychology professor, his experience raising two sons, training at the Michael Gurian Training Institute, as well as extensive experience with boys as a coach, Sunday school teacher, and more. This session shares fresh insights about dealing with boys’ physical aggressiveness, communication needs, and a Biblical approach to their sexuality. This is the first of two workshops which reveals the essential practical strategies drawn from solid research and the speaker’s decades of experience working with boys. You will learn to inspire and enjoy your son more than ever.

I draw on scientific research, my experience raising two sons, as well as extensive volunteer experience with boys as a coach, Sunday school teacher, and more. This session focuses on dealing boys' physical aggressiveness, communication needs, and a Biblical approach to their sexuality.
This is my favorite workshop, and it will draw MANY parents to attend because moms are passionate about their sons and look for insight and help with raising them.

title: Boys R Us: The Key to Your Boy’s Heart
description: Dr. Currie’s workshop draws on his three decades of teaching child and adolescent psychology as well as extensive hands-on experiences with boys and young men. To unlock the treasures in our sons’ hearts, we need to understand what motivates boys. This session will empower your parenting by revealing the three C’s of what drives boys’ behavior. Boys love challenge, conquest, and taking chances. While some believe boys’ energy and exuberance must be stifled, this session will teach you how to celebrate your son and channel his energies for great good. This presentation complements but does not repeat the material from Dr. Currie’s first workshop on the topic of raising sons. Come with questions. Leave with answers and encouragement.

title: Breaking Barriers in Writing: Understanding and Overcoming Dysgraphia
description: When a child struggles to get their thoughts onto paper, it’s often assumed to be a “handwriting problem.” In reality, dysgraphia is rooted in deeper cognitive processes like working memory, processing speed, and language organization. These challenges impact every subject in school and can chip away at confidence. In this workshop, you’ll gain a clear understanding of dysgraphia from a cognitive perspective and learn about brain-based interventions that treat the root causes. Instead of endless handwriting practice, discover how strengthening cognitive skills can open the door to clearer, more confident written expression.

title: Bring History to Life: An Immersive Approach
description: Ignite a love for history in young children by transforming American history lessons into hands-on adventures. We explore how to use all five senses — from cooking and science to art and song — to bring history to life. Learn how sharing the stories of America’s past can inspire your kids with heroic role models, instill traditional values, and be a lot of fun at the same time!

title: Build a Family that Honors God, Honors One Another and Honors Themselves
description: I know sometimes you look at your family and ask yourself, "What's wrong?" Why is there so much conflict? Why is everyone so impatient with each other? Why are we not enjoying one another? Mom, Dad, there is an answer to help unify your family and build a home that is a sanctuary from the world for each of your family members. Come, listen. You will walk away from this workshop with excitement for what is in store for your family!

title: Build an Amazing Homeschool Transcript
description:
(Don’t fear the high school years but learn little-known information that can give your student an edge.)

There is only ONE secret ingredient to giving credibility to a homeschool transcript. If you don’t know author Jean Burk will share the answer in this incredible lecture. Discover the insider criteria that Harvard uses to judge applicants and why summer break needs to be more than just a vacation. Learn the differences between AP, Dual Enrollment, and CLEP courses and the pros and cons of adding them to your schedule. Find out about the 3-Tier credit plan, so you can correctly build a portfolio that will impress any admissions counselor. If your kids are college-bound, you cannot afford to miss this engagement.

title: Build the Story: The Writing Process from a Children’s Author’s Viewpoint
description: Based on her experiences as a writer of children’s mystery novels, nonfiction, and curriculum, Nell Branum shares a glimpse into her writing process and how your child can apply it to his or her own writing. Having guided eight of her grandchildren to publish a book during their third grade year, Nell gives ideas for how your child can develop their own unique writing process.

title: Building a Better Boyhood
description: A revival of daring and heroic boys, resurrecting the boyhoods of ages past, is what our sons need to thrive. Working with your boy’s hardwiring to honor the future man God has called him to be; taking risks, being a leader, serving and sacrificing all begin with letting your wild and noble boy play, explore, and create.

title: Building a Biblical Worldview
description: This is not your standard “Biblical worldview” talk! In fact, Dr. Wile doesn’t think that a Biblical worldview is built with courses and books about worldviews. In Dr. Wile’s opinion, a Biblical worldview is built by studying the Scriptures seriously and studying the academic subjects that relate to Christianity seriously. Thus, he suggests that a student truly needs to build his or her own worldview, rather than being taught what worldview he or she should have. This leads to a Biblical worldview that might be slightly different from his or her parents’ worldview, but it will be one that the student will be more likely to believe throughout the rest of his or her life.

title: Building a Solid Math Foundation — Because Confidence Matters
description: Discover how to set students up for long-term success with a strong foundation rooted in understanding, not memorization.

title: Building Grit: Preparing Kids for Life Challenges
description: A no-nonsense talk on how to toughen up your kids while guarding their hearts.

title: Call to Arms: The Guts & Glory of Courageous Fatherhood
description: There is a spiritual war being waged for our families. Our enemy is strong and we are in his territory. Dad, if you don’t fight for your family, no one else will. When a soldier is in enemy territory, he is alert, focused, and purposeful about every move he makes with weapons ready. Only you stand between the enemy and your family, for God has given you the authority and the responsibility to stop him from destroying your marriage and your children. This takes courage, but your calling is noble…it is God's call to arms. Chuck offers straight answers and practical help for dads who are looking to protect, train, equip, and launch soldiers for Jesus Christ.

title: Can I do this?—Overcoming Homeschool Challenges
description: Does the thought of homeschooling seem as challenging as climbing Mount Everest? With over 17 years of homeschooling 10 children, Cassandra will share the lessons she learned that helped her overcome the biggest challenges.

title: Choosing a College/Major/Career:  It's Better to get it Right than Fast
description: Surveys have shown that 66% of college students change their majors more than once and on the day of graduation 40% of college graduates wish they had chosen a different major, it's important to start early and take this decision seriously. Individuals often make decisions without much thought and with limited information. Come learn about some practical steps to help students explore options that could possibly impact one of the most important decisions of their lives.

title: College Prep 101: Prepare Your Mind for Action by Mastering the Art of Note-taking
description: Let Captain College help transform your academic future forever, whether you are a student or a parent wanting to capture the essence of Life. These extremely practical tips on note-taking will propel your education from a casual learner - like most students, to becoming a Student of Excellence.

title: College Prep 101: Time Management Strategies So You Can Have a Life
description: Time management is THE BIGGEST struggle for all students…and adults. Learn practical ways to get more done in less time so you can get your school work completed, turn in AMAZING projects, and thoroughly enjoy your time on campus.

title: Competing Worldviews of Environmentalism and Christianity
description: The naturalist, pantheist, panenetheist, and animist or spiritist worldviews dominant in mainstream environmentalism conflict with the Biblical worldview that a rational God created an orderly world to be comprehended and ruled by rational human beings made i His image to enhance its fruitfulness, safety, and beauty, to the glory of God and the benefit of our neighbors. This workshop will help homeschooling parents refute the environmentalist worldview(s) and defend the Biblical worldview.

title: Constitution and History Bee!
description: This informative, fun and practical workshop (participants will play games!) will give the home school mom or co-op leader many easy-to-use and free games to teach all subjects, including leadership skills for students of all ages. These games are efficient, time-saving, and kids love them! Learn how to teach history, virtue, patriotism, and Western Civilization, and the Constitution with games.

title: Contention to Calm: The Game of Disagreeing
description: Disagreements are like a game—a game where understanding the rules can turn contention into calm. In this presentation, you’ll learn how to master this game by first understanding the real power of your emotions. Think of them like the game’s hidden rules: once you know how to recognize and process them, they stop controlling you. Next, we’ll focus on communication, showing you how to keep your cool, listen effectively, and avoid climbing the anger ladder that turns a friendly game into an all-out brawl. Finally, we’ll explore the strategy of relationships—because success in any relationship isn’t just about teamwork, but about how you play your role. By the end, you’ll walk away with the skills to play the game of disagreements like a pro, transforming every potential conflict into an opportunity for connection and growth.

title: Cracking the Code of Math Struggles: Understanding and Overcoming Dyscalculia
description: When a child struggles with math—whether it’s recognizing numbers, memorizing math facts, or solving word problems—school quickly becomes discouraging. Many parents are told that dyscalculia, sometimes called “math dyslexia,” is a lifelong condition their child must adapt to. The truth is, there is real help and real hope. In this workshop, you’ll learn how brain research has uncovered the root causes of dyscalculia and math difficulties and discover effective interventions that go beyond endless practice or temporary accommodations. You’ll walk away with practical knowledge and encouragement to help your child gain lasting confidence and success in math.

title: Crafting a Compelling College Entrance Essay
description: Grades get you noticed; essays get you admitted. The personal statement essay—also known as the college entrance essay—is often the tipping point in admissions. It is your chance to share your story, reveal your heart, and rise above the crowd. This is where they get to know you. Most students begin using their personal statement in 11th grade, not only for college admission but are also tailored for scholarship applications. It must be stellar, polished and memorable because it is the most important essay you will write in high school. In this session, students and parents will learn how to transform experiences into powerful essays that admissions officers and scholarship judges remember, gaining both the tools and confidence to write with impact and stand out from the crowd. Test scores and grades will get you considered, but it is the essay that will get you accepted.

title: Create or Die: 7 Keys to Unlocking Creative Potential in Kids
description: Based on Dwayne Walker’s book Create or Die, this talk distills seven timeless keys to unlocking creativity in young minds. You’ll learn how to build an environment where ideas flourish, mistakes become fuel, and imagination becomes your child’s greatest teacher. A must-attend for parents who want creativity to thrive at home.

title: Creating the Ultimate Freedom in Homeschooling
description: The benefits of organization and discipline – perspectives from a homeschool graduate and her mom with practical tips, guides and handouts to adapt to your homeschool.

title: Cultivating Arts of Language: Preschool through High School
description: Listening and reading well, speaking, and writing clearly, thinking, and debating effectively are abilities that most parents hope to cultivate in their children. With that goal in mind, Andrew will explore various environments and activities which will accelerate the development of these language skills, beginning with the youngest students and continuing into the high school years.

title: Cultivating Wonder Throughout the School
Developing and Drafting an SEP (Student Education Plan)
description: Have you pulled your child out of public school where he or she used to have an IEP (Individual Education Plan)? In this workshop, you’ll learn the purposes and benefits of an SEP, the homeschool version of an IEP. You’ll receive guidance on drafting your own SEP, explore sample plans, obtain your own template, and leave with a resource list of books to set you and your student up for success.

title: Discipleship First: Rethinking Life After High School
description: What if the most important decision after high school isn’t a major—but who you’re becoming? We’ll explore why spiritual formation, character development, and real-world skill-building matter more than rushing into a degree. This session will help families think differently about growth, leadership, and launching into adulthood with purpose.

title: Discipleship for Every Age That Reaches the Heart
description: Discipling your children starts from day one and changes over time. In this session, we’ll explore how to consistently, lovingly, and intentionally disciple kids in every stage, from babies to teens. You’ll learn practical ways to foster spiritual growth and lead by example through everyday moments. Whether you’re navigating toddler tantrums or teen questions, this talk will equip you with practical tools and heart-focused strategies to build a Christ-centered home where faith is lived out together.

title: Discipline With Love & Protect Your Relationship With Your Children for Life!
description: Parents, we've all made mistakes in this area. All of us! But, it is so very important that we understand discipline practices that have a greater potential of crushing your child's spirit and/or negatively affecting your relationship with your children long term. Come and be motivated, inspired, comforted and taught by master motivator, Monica Irvine. You really don't want to miss this one!

title: Discipling Your Family in the Essentials of Unity
description: Learn, Apply, and Share what the Bible teaches on key transformational elements for Christian unity that must first be applied in the home, and to then extend to the family of God.

title: Ditch the Word Lists: Spelling That Actually Sticks (and Can Even Be Fun!)
description: This session will challenge the old way of teaching spelling and reveal why endless memorization doesn’t work. If you’ve ever wondered why your child can read fluently but still spells like a beginner, this session will give you the missing piece. You’ll discover a simple, science-backed approach that ends spelling struggles and makes it possible for your kids—not only to succeed—but to actually enjoy spelling.

title: Dollars & Sense
description:
Our time with our children at home is short—but the impact we make lasts a lifetime. As parents, we work hard to teach reading, writing, and math… but what about money skills?
Financial literacy is more than a life skill—it’s a foundation for confidence, independence, and wise decision-making. Yet many kids enter adulthood unprepared for the financial realities ahead.
In this session, we’ll explore practical ways to equip your children with the tools they need to manage money well. From everyday conversations to hands-on strategies, you’ll walk away with tips you can start using right away—no lectures or spreadsheets required.
Join me, a homeschool mom, financial consultant, and retired CPA, as we talk about how to raise kids who are not just smart—but financially savvy.

title: DON’T BE SCARED; BE FEARLESS
description: Many would-be entrepreneurs never flourish because we fear everything from taxes to marketing. Here are the most common hurdles that hold people back and how to overcome them

title: Drawing Near to God in the Midst of a Crazy Life
description: Looking at the life of David, we will learn 5 simple ways to live in peace as we draw near to God in the midst of a crazy life.

title: EARN 1K AN HOUR FOR COLLEGE AND LEARN 30 WAYS TO GO FOR FREE
description: (No fluff, no frills, No joke) You don’t even have to sell a kidney or live off Ramen for life. A debt-free degree is possible; watch your wallet do a happy dance. Our 20 years of college prep experience can save you 20 years of college debt. You will learn secrets to receiving substantial scholarships that go beyond tuition and room and board: paid graduate school, spending cash, money to study abroad, and more! Your students (6th grade and up) can unlock the keys to free college at their fingertips!

title: Educating Twice-Exceptional Students at Home
description: Twice-exceptional children are those who are gifted and have another exceptionality such as OCD or being on the spectrum; there are many strategies for helping these children become highly productive adults.

title: Educational Benefits of Vacationing in a National Park
description: Been to sixty-five national parks, I have a slideshow showing national parks in regions around the country and what history, science, and geography can be discovered during a vacation. I incorporate the Junior Ranger program in my presentation, and share valuable tips for a successful vacation. I show smaller parks that are located near the major ones and the beauty they offer. With 403 parks under the National Park Service umbrella, there will be an option wherever you travel.

title: Engaging the Great Commission: How Students Can Make an Eternal Impact
description: Before Jesus ascended back into Heaven, his final charge to his followers was to take the Gospel to the ends of the earth. With today's world so interconnected, accomplishing this task is easier than ever. In this workshop, From One Child to Another’s co-founder, Natasha Cudd, will share practical strategies for inspiring your kids to utilize the gifts that God has given each one of them to make a difference in their communities and around the world. This session will also include methods for sharing the Gospel with people in your community, opening your student’s eyes to needs of others, and navigating cross-cultural interactions in the States and abroad.

title: Ensure a Solid Foundation in Reading and Spelling (Pre-K - 2nd Grade)
speakerName: Deede Cauley, M.Ed., LPC, LMFT, FSAFBI

title: Ensure Excellence in Reading, Spelling & Comprehension (3-12th Grades)
speakerName: Deede Cauley, M.Ed., LPC, LMFT, FSAFBI
description:
Looking for a reading curriculum to start your child off right? Concerned your child is “reading” by looking at the pictures, guessing the words, or might be getting behind? Gain insight into why some students dislike, struggle, or lack focus when reading – including but not limited to those with learning disabilities. This workshop will include a brief discussion of recent brain research on building neural networking and how a parent can easily change the way his or her student processes information.
The RSPS creator will provide you with confidence and specific and simple things you can do so that your children will be good readers who have strong comprehension skills, an excellent vocabulary, and the ability to accurately spell. There is little that is more important than that a child develops the ability to become an independent reader, thinker, and communicator. RSPS provides a solid foundation that can be used with any curriculum a parent might choose.

title: Entrepreneurship for Homeschool Teens: Turning Ideas into Income
description: Has your teen ever dreamed of starting their own business—or wondered if that’s something they could actually do before or after graduation? In this session, we’ll explore real ways homeschool teens can launch small businesses, from simple side hustles to buying existing ventures. You’ll learn what makes young entrepreneurs successful, what challenges to expect, and how to use business as a hands-on learning experience.

title: Everyone Can (and Should!) Learn to Play Music
description:
Did you know that cows produce more milk when they listen to Mozart? This may sound like udder nonsense, but it’s true! And did you know plants grow stronger and faster if you serenade them with your violin?
But enough with the cows and plants (for now). The effects of music on human beings are simply astounding. Music can reduce pain, heal physical and psychological ailments, improve memory and concentration, relieve depression and anxiety, and plenty more.
Want the maximum physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual benefits? Learn an instrument! “There’s not a musical bone in my body.” Nah, you got this. Join me for a fascinating rediscovery of the blessings of music, while also grabbing a few tips for how to successfully learn an instrument...even if you’re not "musically inclined." (Notes—high and low—will be provided at the workshop.)

title: EZ+1: The IEW Method of Teaching
Family Business 101: Teaching Kids Entrepreneurship at Any Age
description: What if you could turn everyday lessons into real-world business skills for your kids? In this hands-on workshop, you’ll walk through the basics of launching a small business right alongside your children . We’ll start by creating a sample business together, then break down a simple business plan to see what makes an idea viable . Using practical examples—from service industries to cost of goods—we’ll explore income potential, legal structures, and the lessons learned from both success and failure . Whether you’re raising future entrepreneurs or simply want to instill financial and life skills, this workshop will give you tools to start today .

title: Fantasy and the Reader's Heart
description: How fantasy can inspire readers in their spiritual lives, and how to discern good, moral fantasy and identify destructive fantasy. The workshop also provides an extensive list of Christian world view fantasy authors to choose from as well as online resources for parents’ guides to popular books. This is a helpful talk for parents, teachers, and other adults who guide children in their literary pursuits.

title: Fearfully and Wonderfully Made: Using the Gift of Neuroplasticity to Overcome Learning Difficulties
description: Homeschooling children with learning difficulties presents some unique challenges. The great news is that all of us were created with the gift of neuroplasticity which allows us to rewire the brain to overcome learning difficulties. Our brains are malleable, moldable, and changeable. Join us as we cover how to harness neuroplasticity to help your child learn at optimal levels and reach their full potential.

title: Fearless Fevers: The Fever Class Every Homeschool Family Needs
description: Today, many parents know—at least in theory—that fevers can be a good thing. But when the numbers on the thermometer climb higher than we expect, panic often takes over. We’re living in a world where fevers are feared, suppressed, and misunderstood. And yet, our bodies—especially our children’s bodies—were designed by our Creator with incredible healing intelligence. When we understand what a fever truly is and how it works, something changes inside of us. Fear is replaced with calm, confusion is replaced with clarity, and we finally see illness the way God intended us to see it: as a pathway toward greater strength and resiliency. In this workshop, Paola will demystify the fever once and for all. You’ll learn why fevers occur, what they accomplish, and the simple patterns parents can watch for to recognize whether their child is heading toward healing or deeper into imbalance. You’ll also gain a clear sense of when to observe, when to support, and when to get help—without second-guessing yourself at every turn.

title: Field Trips Can Change Your Life: What History Museums Have to Offer
description: Field trips don’t have to end when you leave a museum. Learn about special, often free, resources museums can offer, including local/state history, student competitions, and primary sources.

title: Fight the Lion: A Call to Fathers for Spiritual Warfare
description:
1st Peter 5:8 reminds us that the enemy prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour. The enemy is targeting our homes, our marriages, and our children. In this urgent and inspiring session, Pastor Trey issues a challenge to fathers: it’s time to stand in the gap, take up spiritual arms, and battle for the souls of your family. It's time to Fight the Lion!
Pastor Trey's goal for this session is to deliver a rallying cry before an army of fathers preparing to do spiritual warfare for the sake of their families. With biblical clarity and passionate conviction, Trey will challenge and equip dads to become the spiritual warriors God has called them to be.

title: Fighting Apathy: Teaching Your Teens to Care
description: More than ever before, raising teenagers that care about their faith is hard, especially in a culture that is quickly becoming increasingly hostile toward Christians. When a child enters those confusing years, the enemy uses dozens of weapons to rob them of their faith. Spiritual apathy is insidious and perhaps the most destructive of all. Chuck and his wife raised six children in this age of technology, learning to fight back against teen apathy and ultimately helping their children’s faith not only survive, but thrive. Chuck shares understanding and techniques to equip parents for these challenging teen years.

title: Finding Good Stories
description: With so many stories being produced each year it can feel impossible to find GOOD stories. Sifting through the masses is tedious and exhausting. But what really makes a story GOOD? Christian Hauer, author of “The Bygone Sagas of Oogerel” and “The Marvelous Mr Motley”, discusses key points to look for when analyzing stories to find the Truth within and how to analyze with your kids whether a story is good or bad.

title: Five Flavors of Homescholing
description: The world of homeschooling is a lot like the world of food. You know that you want to provide nutritious meals for your children’s minds, but there are so many possibilities for what that food could look like and taste like. Join Sonya for a simple overview of five main flavors of homeschooling: traditional, classical, Charlotte Mason, unit studies, unschooling. Find your preferred flavor and learn how to shop for a successful homeschool menu.

title: Five Key Points Every American Needs to Know about American History
description:
Five Key Points Every American Needs to Know about American History, And How You Can Teach it to Kids, ages 5-18

Although it appears Americans have turned the corner and begun to triumph over revisionism, fake history, and wokeism, there still is a great lack of history knowledge among our children and adults. John De Gree will use his new book, American History, the Story of Liberty! America’s Heritage through the Reagan Revolution, and explain five key points every American needs to know about American history to understand how our country is exceptional, inspiring, and worth fighting for.

title: For Those Moments When You Don't Like Your Child (But You Still Love Them)
description: No one wants to admit it, but most every parent has that moment (or moments) where they're having a difficult time liking their child. It usually has something to do with the destruction of your personal property, or a recurring character flaw (like dishonesty), or the fact that you both have personality traits that are uncomfortably similar. Whatever the cause, left unchecked these feelings can grow into something much stronger than mere "dislike". In this talk we'll explore the root cause of these feelings and offer some practical advice to help you avoid the situations that bring them about.

title: For Those Moments When You Don't Like Your Child (But You Still Love Them)
Force Won't Fix It
Foreign Languages: Where do I Start?
description: This workshop covers everything from which language to choose, what ages, how to teach multiple ages, and which curriculum will work best for your family. Each attendee will leave with a rubric and flowchart to help them decide what EXACTLY is their first step in this intimidating curriculum area.

title: Forging a Brighter Path for children with Scoliosis
description: Revolutionary approach to treating Scoliosis-Scoliosis Activity suit, DNA and clinical testing to pinpoint and address root causes of Idiopathic scoliosis through tailored nutrient therapies.

title: Fractions Are Your Friends!
description:
We often hear from students and parents that they don’t like fractions. What if fractions were learned in a way that gives the student a way to discover relationships that exist in math making math not only fractions easier, but all math concepts easier? Participants will leave the session understanding the parts of a fraction and the function of each part. Participants will discover the answer to questions like: Why do you need a common denominator when adding
fractions? How can you add, subtract, multiply or divide fractions using the same method whether the fraction has, numbers, letters (variables) or both? Participants will learn how to translate common English language used when speaking about fractions into math symbols that make sense and can be applied at any time.

title: From Discipline to Discipleship: Raising Kids with Eternity in Mind
description: As Christian children grow, so should the role of their parents. There comes a pivotal point in parenting when the focus must shift—from correcting behavior to cultivating belief. In this powerful session, Pastor Trey helps parents navigate the transition from being the disciplinarian to becoming a disciple-maker. With biblical wisdom and practical tools, he'll equip you to go beyond raising well-mannered teens and start intentionally shaping Christ-following disciples.

title: From Frustration to Focus: A Functional Medicine Approach to ADHD & Homeschooling
description: Discover a functional medicine lens on ADHD and homeschooling—uncover root causes, rethink focus, and build brain-friendly learning environments that help your child thrive without labels, meds, or overwhelm.

title: From Museum to Math: Teaching Core Subjects Through Travel
description: From Museum to Math: Teaching Core Subjects Through Travel shows homeschool families how everyday trips can become powerful learning experiences. This workshop explores how museums, parks, historic sites, and simple road trips naturally teach language arts, math, science, social studies, and the arts—without adding more worksheets or stress. Attendees will learn how to spot learning opportunities before, during, and after travel, adapt activities for different ages and learning styles, and document experiences for portfolios and transcripts. With practical examples and simple strategies, families will leave encouraged to see every destination as a classroom and every trip as a chance to learn.

title: From Passion to Profession: The Story of a Homeschooled Entrepreneur
description:
How do you turn a passion into a career? Homeschool graduate Millie Florence shares her story, from self-publishing her first novel at age thirteen to writing full-time at age twenty – and the four principles and practices that got her there. Also, why she couldn’t have done it without elevators and musical theater.
This presentation will inspire kids to chase their dreams, writing or otherwise, and give parents an inside look at how to support them. We’ll have about 25 minutes of Q&A at the end, so bring your homeschooling and entrepreneurship questions!

title: From The Lion King to The King of Kings
description: Davy Liu, a former Disney animator behind films like The Lion King and Beauty and the Beast, discovered a higher calling while working on Star Wars. He left Disney to serve the Lord, founding Kendu Films in 2004 to create Christ-centered content for children. His award-winning Bible picture books are now being adapted into animated features, bringing the gospel to life through storytelling and exceptional artistry.

title: From the True to the Good: The Role of Logic in Human Flourishing
description:
From the True to the Good: The Role of Logic in Human Flourishing
Classical education has long held that the pursuit of the Good Life requires more than moral aspiration—it requires trained judgment. In this session, we will explore how the study of logic in the middle-grade years (7th–10th) forms the habits of mind necessary for discerning truth, resisting error, and choosing what is genuinely good. Logic is often misunderstood as a merely technical or academic subject, but in the classical tradition it is a moral art, shaping students to love what is true so they may pursue lives ordered toward the good. Drawing from classical sources, contemporary reflections on flourishing, and practical experience teaching logic, this session will show how logic strengthens prudence, cultivates intellectual virtue, and supports a vision of human flourishing rooted in the True, the Good, and the Beautiful. Participants will leave with a renewed understanding of logic’s formative power and practical ways to connect logic instruction to the moral aims of classical education.

title: From Wonder to Mastery
Gen Z Unfiltered: Redefining Success, School, and the Skilled Trades
description: Four-year college isn't the dream for every Gen Z student—some want skills, not syllabi. In this high-energy session, we’ll dig into why the skilled trades are hotter than ever, what success means to Gen Z, and how you can connect your students to real career paths they’ve probably never considered. You’ll also get a sneak peek at SPARK, the chatbot built to answer student questions about skilled trades, jobs, and training—so you don’t have to.

title: Getting to The Heart
description: This session will encourage parents to focus on their child's heart and spiritual growth in the midst of their homeschool day. They will learn how to weave everyday homeschool moments into discipleship opportunities. Parents will leave equipped and encouraged to disciple their children lovingly, authentically, consistently, and intentionally, focusing on their hearts and encouraging a love for Jesus and others.

title: God is Calling His People to STAND UP and SPEAK UP! IF Not YOU, WHO?
description: Monica Irvine comes to us with decades of experience standing up and speaking up for truth, for America, and for the Children. Has the Lord been tapping your shoulder lately, trying to get you to get more involved in what is happening in your community, in your state or even in America? Are you scared? Are you tempted to think “How could someone like me make a difference?” Are you ignoring the promptings of the spirit to “Get Involved?” Come, listen to master motivator and activist, Monica Irvine, talk about the “CALL” and how you can answer it, so that by your obedience to the Lord, you truly can make a difference. Don’t miss this! It could change more than you, it could change the world.

title: God's Design for Marital Roles
description:
In a world full of confusion about marriage, God’s Word offers clarity, purpose, and peace. This workshop cuts through the noise and gets straight to the heart of His design.
Together we will explore:
What does Godly masculinity really look like?
What does Godly femininity look like—and why does it matter?
Why did God give husbands and wives distinct roles?
How do these roles work together to create a strong, unified marriage?
Direct, biblical, practical. This session invites you to rediscover God’s blueprint and why it still transforms marriages today.

title: Goldilocks Discipline: How to Find a Just-Right Approach for Your Family
description: There are lots of experts who will tell you the “right way” to discipline your child. But is it the right way for your family? In this session, you’ll learn why parents are attracted to discipline strategies that don’t work long-term and how to change your approach in a way that feels good for every family member.

title: Good Writers vs Great Writers
description: We’d all like our children to become good writers, but what if I told you there are some keys to helping them become great writers? Great writers don’t merely punctuate correctly and paraphrase what someone else has written. Great writers know how to clearly express their own ideas in their own unique writing voice. Teaching structure is important, and encouraging style is good, but challenging your child to elevate their writing to the next level and become a wordsmith is a gift. Using timeless tools from both classical and Charlotte Mason traditions, learn tips to transform your good writer into a great writer.

title: Grow the Man, Don’t Break the Boy
description: Kelly Love, a father of three boys, shares practical strategies for raising boys into strong, confident men without breaking their spirit. Learn how to balance discipline with individuality, build trust while teaching responsibility, and encourage integrity and compassion. This talk offers clear, actionable advice for parents, mentors, and educators looking to guide boys toward their full potential with care and purpose.

title: Hacking High School…and College!?!
description: Andrew Pudewa & Nicholas Ellis (IEW & Christian Halls International)

title: Hacking High School…and College!?!
description: Andrew Pudewa & Nicholas Ellis (IEW & Christian Halls International)

title: Hands-On Science with TOPS Learning Systems
description: Let's learn about hands-on, student directed science labs using household materials. We'll discuss how to create a customized curriculum for your school year.

title: Harnessing the Power of Imagination to Exercise
description: We will demonstrate the core of what the Superhero Training Academy is trying to accomplish it and how we do it. By using the child's imaginations, we utilize technology to get them moving and exercising. They don't realize that they're in a routine of regular exercise, but instead are sucked into a story that they have fun participating in. We'll discuss the why of this being necessary and also the benefits parents see by participating.

title: Help! My Child Is Struggling With Writing
description: Being an effective student writer is not the norm. In fact, most students struggle with academic writing. Yet, as parents, we all know that writing clearly is key to our children's success. In this workshop, you'll learn about why being able to write clearly is so important in our fast-paced digital world and how to help your kids get up to speed if they're behind regarding this all-important skill.

title: Helping Mom Craft a Creative and Thoughtfully Designed Curriculum
description: Is your head spinning with all the homeschooling lingo? Charlotte Mason. Classical. Unschooling. Traditional. Are you trying to pick and choose but don’t know what would be the best fit for your family? Do you tend to lean towards thinking outside the box? Or do you feel stuck in the box without any other options? Come discover why one style does not have to fit all. Depending upon your child’s bent, age, grade, learning challenges or interests, mom can a la carte with purpose. Julia Nalle shares from teaching her own children and through designing the BiblioPlan curriculum that it is possible to thoughtfully pick and choose a little bit of this and a little bit of that - with success.

title: High School Reimagined: Purpose & Preparation for Success After Graduation
description: It’s time to rethink high school so your student can focus on a strategized and purposeful education that allows time for career exploration, leadership development, life skills, extracurricular activities, volunteering, and internships. Students replace long lectures and endless busywork with short, mastery-based lessons while building critical thinking skills, academic confidence, and independence. Imagine your teen enjoying high school, finding their strengths, and being prepared for success after graduation.

title: Holistic Health for the Family
description: Diving into the key principles of holistic health, participants will gain practical, research-informed insight into how herbs and nutrient-dense foods can be used to promote whole-body health along with the importance of eliminating toxins. Leave with specific ways to utilize natural resources to support brain and gut health, your immune system, and the connection between emotions, stress, and physical well-being.

title: Home Education in Ohio: Which State Policies are Fixed or in Flux?
description: Are you familiar with Ohio’s state statutes that directly or indirectly affect home educating families? Are you sure you are making the most of the opportunities available for your children? This workshop will review the nuances in state law that will help to guide your planning from the elementary years through high school and beyond.

title: Homeopathy for First Aid: A Workshop for Active, Adventurous Homeschool Families
description: Every family has those moments—the bike crashes on the gravel road, the slip on the creek bank, the tumble off the trampoline, the slammed finger in the doorway, or the mysterious swelling that seems to appear out of nowhere. Raising children means raising little explorers, and if your home is anything like mine, your kids are constantly climbing, jumping, adventuring, and testing the limits of gravity and common sense. For homeschool families in particular, our kids spend more time outdoors, learning by doing, hands-on, boots-in-the-mud, heart-wide-open. With that kind of lifestyle comes the occasional bump, bruise, cut, twist, sting, or scare. The question is never if something will happen—it’s when. And when it does, we as parents want to feel steady, confident, and equipped. In this workshop, we will walk through the foundational first aid skills every family should know—paired with the gentle, powerful support of homeopathy. Whether you’re dealing with a sprained ankle during nature study, a burn from a kitchen experiment gone wild, or a bruised knee from a backyard obstacle course, homeopathy offers simple, safe tools that help the body repair itself quickly and naturally.

title: Homeschooling Gifted Children Successfully
description: Gifted children present unique challenges for parents; this workshop presents many proven strategies for meeting the challenges.

title: Homeschooling: A Guide to Choosing and Structuring Curriculum from  Kindergarten to High School (Spanish)
description: One of the biggest concerns for Hispanic parents is how to choose the right curriculum for their children. In this workshop, we’ll explore the key subjects and skills children should develop throughout the school year. We’ll also discuss how to adapt the curriculum if English is your second language or if you want to provide a bilingual education. You’ll learn how to plan your educational content, set clear goals based on your family’s needs, and nurture the values and skills your children need for a successful future

title: Homeschooling: How to Start with Confidence and Finish Successfully (Spanish)
description: In this workshop, we’ll address the most common concerns parents face when considering homeschooling: what it is, how it differs from traditional education, how to get started, how to build an effective routine, and how to evaluate progress to finish strong. You’ll gain practical tools to transform your home into a positive and effective learning environment.

title: How American Children Won World War II
description: Are we as parents and leaders ever reluctant to allow our children to do important things? Perhaps we find ourselves questioning their abilities or desire to serve others? Let’s turn back the calendar to the 1940s wartime period to see how a previous generation of children was invited to make a difference!

title: How Brain Development Drives Behaviors
description: Educating parents on how brain develops, and now gaps in development can cause challenging behaviors.

title: How Can My Children Be Successful Learning Another Language At Home?
description: Learning a second language is challenging but can it be done successfully at home if I’m not proficient in another language? This topic is thoroughly explored and explained in this interesting and helpful talk. We will discuss the various methods, programs and practices available to teach this very necessary subject FOR ALL AGES!

title: How Liberals Hijacked American Education
How the Brain Works Part 1: Strategies for Effective Learning
description: Did you know your child’s brain is a masterpiece of potential just waiting to be unlocked? In this dynamic and engaging seminar, we’ll dive into the brain’s fascinating design and uncover practical ways to harness its incredible capabilities. Discover why timing is everything, how emotions influence memory, and why self-control is a secret superpower. Get ready to laugh, learn, and even involve the kids in this fast-paced session that’s as fun as it is informative. Let’s inspire excellence, one brain at a time!

title: How the Brain Works Part 2: Strategies for Effective Learning
description: In part two of my How the Brain Works seminar we will build on the basics from part one and dive into the power of mindset, including focus, the magic of goal-setting, and how your words can motivate and build up or unintentionally tear down. Discover how to maximize your child’s learning potential, build confidence, and cultivate a mindset that drives purpose and excellence. This session is packed with tips, tricks, scriptural insights, and plenty of "aha!" moments to make you a brain-powered parenting pro!

title: How the Christian Worldview Can Rescue Science from Irrationalism
description: Secularist science's abandonment of the Biblical worldview and embrace of naturalism has led to scientific irrationalism in the form of post-normal science. This workshop will help homeschooling parents teach how Christian theism, with Biblical propositional revelation and logic (from the Logos who lightens everyone who comes into the world) can restore sane, rational science.

title: How the Radical Left Conquered Education, and How You Can Save Your Child and America
description: John De Gree explains how America’s educational system, bureaucracy, media, and much of government has come under control of the Radical Left. He then details how you, the home school-teacher, co-op director, or charter school leader, can save your students from being brainwashed to believing and following the leftist ideology that has captured much of our country.

title: How to Ace the SAT/ACT/CLT
description:
Discover how this is a learnable test and every question can be answered in 30 seconds or less.

Test-prep Guru, Jean Burk, will share her secrets of 20 years of test prep success that have helped tens of thousands of students raise their SAT scores as much as 700 points, ACT 11 points, CLT 26 points! Learn how to find shortcuts in test patterns and save time on all types of questions. Math, Writing, Reading, and Science are all covered in this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity. Discover that testing is here to stay. Learn why test scores are tied directly to scholarships. If you have college-bound students, you can’t miss this seminar about preparing them for the SAT, CLT and ACT!

title: How to Build Real-World Skills Through Entrepreneurship & Internships
description:
Prepare your students for life beyond the classroom! In this empowering session, veteran educator and homeschool consultant Tricia Siletti shares how to integrate entrepreneurship and internships into your homeschool curriculum to build confidence, responsibility, and real-world skills. Learn how to guide your children in launching microbusinesses, developing products, managing finances, and participating in meaningful internships that align with their passions. Discover practical tools to track learning for transcripts and college readiness—while raising independent, capable young adults who know how to think, create, and lead.
Learning Objectives:
Learn how to design entrepreneurship projects that build financial and critical-thinking skills
Explore ways to connect teens with internships and mentorships that reflect their interests
Understand how to document business and internship experiences for transcripts or portfolios
Gain step-by-step tools to foster independence, accountability, and creativity in students

title: How to Build Self-Regulating Families in an Emotional World: Teaching Calmness to Children
description: What if your children could learn to calm themselves—without yelling, power struggles, or constant parental intervention? In this practical and hope-filled class, Nicholeen Peck gives parents a clear roadmap for raising confident, self-governing children in a world where emotions are often overwhelming. Whether you’re raising toddlers, teens, or neurodiverse children with big emotions, you’ll discover a simple process to help children at every age build real emotional regulation and how to help your child interrupt this loop before the meltdown. You’ll leave empowered, equipped, and ready to help your family thrive.

title: How to Help Your Child Understand Salvation, Death, and the Hereafter
description: When Christians are asked the question, “What Happens When You Die” a recent study shows that many Christians do not really know. Some say, “They hope they go to Heaven,” others have even believed in reincarnation. This presentation explains salvation so that a person will know if they are going to heaven, why death occurs, what really happens after we die and it is not just going to heaven and staying there. It also goes into what happens when a non-believer dies. It is an overview of the book written by the speaker Dr. Karen S. Miller, “Nobody is Talking about The Dead Elephant in the Room; How to Overcome the Fear of Death

title: How To Help Your Math Struggler
description: A college math lecturer wants to help you to know how to help your math struggler. As the parent, learn what you need to know about mathematics. Find the key principles your math struggler needs in a math curriculum (and what they don't). Understand the emotional side of math struggles and what you can do to help. Don't let the problem go unsolved.

title: How to Homeschool As You Travel
description: So, you’ve booked the trip… now what? In this actionable workshop, you’ll map out how to homeschool before, during, and after your travel experience—without losing momentum or missing opportunities for deep learning. Whether you’re road tripping across your state or flying across the ocean, you’ll define your travel vision, align your curriculum to your goals, and discover how to turn every part of the journey into meaningful education. Get ready to create a homeschool rhythm that thrives on the road—and builds lasting memories every step of the way.

title: How to Outthink, Outsmart & Outlast the Strong-Willed Homeschooler
description: Homeschooling parents are often fearful of the government or homeschool haters. But they’re not the real threat. The real threat is in your house: a strong-willed homeschooler that you’re unprepared for. If your kids are sweet, submissive cherubs, you don’t need this session. Otherwise, you’ll want to join psychologist and homeschool survivor, Dr. Melanie Wilson, to learn the strategies.

title: How to Prepare Your Kids for the AI Revolution: Future-Ready Skills
description: Artificial Intelligence is changing everything—from college to careers to how we learn. But homeschoolers are perfectly positioned to thrive. In this upbeat, practical session, Lisa Nehring reveals the 5 C’s of Future-Ready Education—Creativity, Critical Thinking, Communication, Collaboration, and Character—and how to weave them naturally into your homeschool. You’ll leave inspired, equipped, and confident about raising adaptable, faith-filled kids who can’t be replaced by robots—and ready to lead in tomorrow’s world.

title: How to Program a Robot
description: In this fun and interactive STEM presentation, students will help program a robot to perform tasks. Through this engaging process students learn fundamentals of programming, troubleshooting skills, and components that impact the robot’s performance. Students learn how hardware & software work together for robots and all computerized devices.

title: How to Spot the Real Enemy: Discernment in Fantasy and Faith
description:
Fantasy Novels - Understand good vs evil in a relatable, spiritual way.

Explore how fantasy stories can become powerful tools for teaching biblical discernment. Learn how to help your children recognize truth vs. deception, identify healthy vs. harmful themes, and see spiritual principles reflected in relatable, imaginative worlds. We’ll discuss how to guide young readers in spotting the real enemy, pride, fear, lies, temptation, and how to frame fantasy as an opportunity for conversations about virtue, courage, faith, and the light that overcomes darkness.

title: How to Take a Stand - At a Young Age!
description: There are so many issues you may see up-ending the moral fabric of our nation, and you may be asking, "what can I do about it"? We will educate you on what to do, show you historical examples of God using the young people to change the course of a nation, and empower you to do just that.

title: How to Teach Early Literacy Skills through Play
description: Have a toddler? Preschooler? Rising K5? No curriculum needed - they're too young! In this workshop we'll discuss exactly what you can be doing to effectively introduce and practice essential early reading skills through hands on materials and play. Workbooks and scripted curricula can be saved for later, let's ensure our little ones are ready to become readers by engaging in purposeful play.

title: How to Turn Hidden Homeschooling Expertise Into Income
description: Everyone has knowledge that others would benefit from—they just don’t realize it. In this session, teens and parents will learn how to turn their knowledge and passions into digital courses that generate passive income and long-term financial freedom. This session covers the process of identifying your hidden expertise, building a course and launching it—while learning basic skills in marketing, entrepreneurship and financial independence. You don’t have to wait until after college or after homeschooling to start building income streams. You can start today!

title: How to Write a Hero's Journey Story
description: An interactive writing lesson on how to hook readers at the start of a hero’s journey story and keep them engaged. This is a great workshop for aspiring writers, because it helps them jump-start a story by avoiding the frustrations that accompany staring at a blank page. It is also helpful for homeschool teachers who want ideas on how to effectively teach writing skills.

title: How Vision Shapes Learning: The Link Behind Reading, Writing, & Focus
speakerName: Dr. Rosalie Hopkins OD, FAAO, FOVDR
description:
Most parents and educators know that good eyesight matters—but a minority realize that functional vision skills are the foundation for nearly every part of learning. In this session, you’ll discover how the eyes and brain work together to support reading fluency, handwriting, comprehension, attention, and even behavior. We’ll break down the difference between 20/20 eyesight and the functional visual skills required for school success, including tracking, focusing, eye teaming, visual perception, and visual-motor integration.
You’ll learn how hidden vision problems can mimic ADHD, dyslexia, or “not trying,” and why struggles like losing place, skipping lines, big or tiny handwriting, slow reading, or difficulty focusing often have a visual cause. Parents will walk away with practical tools, simple-at-home screenings, and clear guidance on when a child may benefit from a developmental vision evaluation.
Whether your child is thriving, struggling or somewhere in between, this session will open your eyes to how vision truly shapes learning—and how improving visual skills can transform your homeschool experience.

title: Ice Cream with Authors Live: A Special Podcast Experience for Families
description:
Join award-winning Christian middle grade authors Becca Wierwille and Amanda Trumpower, the hosts of Ice Cream with Authors, a Christian book podcast for kids. We’re hosting a one-of-a-kind live episode right from the convention!

You'll be part of a live studio audience as we interview a surprise guest who writes faith-filled, family-friendly stories. Your kids will love the chance to see a real podcast recording in action, ask questions, and participate in storytelling games.

Why attend? Because it’s more than just listening to a podcast—it's an interactive, engaging event that focuses on finding good Christian literature that aligns with traditional values. Your family will leave inspired and encouraged. Plus, the episode we record will later air on our show, so you might just hear yourself on Ice Cream with Authors!

Come for the stories, stay for the fellowship, and leave with a little more sweetness in your homeschool journey. We can’t wait to see you there! (Ice cream not included.)

title: Ice Cream with Authors Live: A Special Podcast Experience for Families
description:
Join award-winning Christian middle grade authors Becca Wierwille and Amanda Trumpower, the hosts of Ice Cream with Authors, a Christian book podcast for kids. We’re hosting a one-of-a-kind live episode right from the convention!

You'll be part of a live studio audience as we interview a surprise guest who writes faith-filled, family-friendly stories. Your kids will love the chance to see a real podcast recording in action, ask questions, and participate in storytelling games.

Why attend? Because it’s more than just listening to a podcast—it's an interactive, engaging event that focuses on finding good Christian literature that aligns with traditional values. Your family will leave inspired and encouraged. Plus, the episode we record will later air on our show, so you might just hear yourself on Ice Cream with Authors!

Come for the stories, stay for the fellowship, and leave with a little more sweetness in your homeschool journey. We can’t wait to see you there! (Ice cream not included.)

title: If You Could See How I See: Insights from Life on the Autism Spectrum
description:
In this encouraging and eye-opening session, an autistic adult shares personal stories and hard-won insights to help parents better understand how their child thinks, feels, and experiences the world. Rather than viewing autistic traits as obstacles, this presentation reframes them as meaningful signals—rooted in sensory processing, interoception, and a brain that works differently, not incorrectly.
Through authentic examples—including moments of childhood confusion, communication challenges, and even humorous sensory misunderstandings—participants will gain a clearer picture of what is happening beneath behaviors that may seem puzzling or overwhelming. The session highlights how small shifts in communication, such as using short, direct questions rather than open-ended ones, can help you “translate” your child’s inner world more accurately. The session also highlights resources and supports available through The Learning Gardens, including parent coaching.
