Parents will leave with renewed hope, practical language, and simple strategies for strengthening connection—and a deeper appreciation for the remarkable thinking of children across the ASD spectrum.

title: IMMUNIZATION FROM THE SYSTEM
description: We live in unstable and vulnerable times. Dysfunction is like a sickness. Temptation, dependence, and values collapse jeopardize all of us in our efforts to be shining lights of truth in our world. Here are suggestions to maintain robust immunity from a Godless system.

title: Is your child using AI effectively? A Parent’s guide to learning tools
description:
More and more homeschool parents are asking tough, honest questions:
“Is my child using AI the right way?”
“Are they actually learning… or just letting AI do the work?”
“Should we even be using AI in our homeschool?”
“What if AI becomes a crutch instead of a tool?”
Some families feel excited about AI, while others feel apprehensive or unsure where to even begin. This workshop speaks directly to both sides — whether your child is already using AI daily, or you’ve been hesitant to introduce it at all.
We’ll break down what effective, healthy AI use looks like in a learning environment and answer the very real concerns parents have today. Through practical examples and demonstrations, you’ll clearly see:
-How AI can support learning — helping students brainstorm, explore concepts, practice critical thinking, and debug code
-When AI crosses the line into harmful shortcuts that prevent true understanding
-How to tell if your child is relying on AI or actually building skills
-What responsible, age-appropriate AI habits look like for homeschoolers
-Why avoiding AI entirely may leave students at a disadvantage later on
-How to safely introduce (or re-introduce) AI into your homeschool as a learning enhancer, not a replacement.
You’ll leave with practical strategies, simple checklists, and confidence — whether your goal is to help your child use AI more effectively or to finally answer the question:
“Should we be using AI in our homeschool… and if so, how?”
This session brings clarity, balance, and real-world guidance to a topic many parents feel overwhelmed by — grounded in the same practices we use at MYTEK LAB to help students grow in creativity, independence, and future-ready skills.

title: KEEPERS OF THE BOOKS: The Vital Importance of a Home Library
description: Throughout history, reading and owning books has been associated with culture, learning, and wisdom. In modern times, books have yielded to electronic information systems. Jan, a vintage bookseller, believes that the older books, written true to a Christian worldview and absent many modern biases, need to be rescued from destruction, read by the students of today, and kept safe for future generations. Jan gives guidelines on choosing which books to include in a home library, which books to borrow from a public library, and which books to send to the trash. Practical suggestions on book repair, deodorizing, and organizing will also be discussed.

title: Latin, Greek, Hebrew--Why your children should and how they can read them
description:
When Harvard was founded in 1636, Latin was the primary language of instruction. The Yale valedictorians of classes 1785 and 1792 delivered their addresses in Hebrew. Even as late as 1857, a Greek salutatory oration was given at the University of Pennsylvania's commencement ceremony. Latin, Greek, and Hebrew had a central role in the education of Americans for the first 200+ years. These languages are now hardly studied and very few gain useful proficiency in them.
I make a case for why learning these three ancient languages is so important for our culture. After showing their importance, I introduce modern learning science theories and how they can transform learning efficiency in any domain. I demonstrate how such theories can be applied to Latin, Greek, and Hebrew, so that your child (and you!) can learn these languages at any age and in the most efficient way possible (as a personal testimony, my son was reading Hebrew at 4). As a homeschool dad myself, I also discuss my firsthand experience of teaching my own kids Greek and Hebrew.

title: Lauching Your Homeschool Journey - Success for the Road
description: Homeschooling is a journey as unique as each family that embraces it. Whether you're just starting to explore this educational path or you're a seasoned veteran, there's always an opportunity to learn, grow,and refine your approach. Join Gretchen Roe for an inspiring presentation filled with invaluable, practical tips and essential tools. This session is designed to empower every family, regardless of their experience level, to navigate the challenges, celebrate the triumphs, and discover lasting joy, efficiency, and success in their unique homeschooling environment.

title: Laying Down the Rails: The Power of Good Habits in Your Home School
description:
Discover the secret to smooth and easy days: habit training. Learn how to instill good habits, which habits Charlotte Mason encouraged parents to cultivate in their children, and practical tips for cultivating the Top Three.

Laying Down the Rails: The Power of Good Habits in Your Home School

title: Lead So They Listen: Restoring Roles, Respect, and Family Identity
description: When the world shifts, home must hold. Learn how strong parental leadership and unified family standards anchor your children, build strong relationships, and restore healthy family roles. In this powerful class, Nicholeen Peck teaches the proactive relationship skills that inspire confidence and respect, strengthen identity, and unite your family with purpose in an unpredictable world.

title: Learn the Greek Alphabet in an Hour
description: Latin and Greek are valuable additions to any student’s academic program, but many students shy away from Greek because it uses a different alphabet. Yet the Greek alphabet is the basis of the Latin, and learning it is not as hard as you might think. In this popular workshop, Regan Barr demystifies the Greek alphabet with a quick phonetic introduction, teaching the letters in groups. Then he challenges the audience to use their newly acquired knowledge through frequent “pop quizzes” that make this workshop interactive and fun.

title: Learning Languages through Ages and Stages
description:
Different Ages react differently to learning a language. Learn all the effective, tested methods and resources right here, and know where to look, and how to adjust to make learning a language simple and fun, and doable for both you and your children.

The Key to Learning Anything will be a freebie in this seminar. Come and grab your free ebook as well as a fool proof way to discover your learning style, your child's learning style, and what that means for your homeschool days.

title: Learning Like a Detective: Science and History Through Mystery Stories
description:
How do real scientists, historians, and detectives think? They observe, ask questions, collect evidence, test ideas, and revise their conclusions. In other words—they solve mysteries.
In this engaging workshop, children’s author and educator Stephanie Bearce shows homeschool parents how mystery novels can become powerful tools for teaching science, history, and critical thinking. Using examples from the Raven Gallows Mysteries, including The Secret of Moonrise Manor and The Curse of the Lost Cave, parents will discover how story-driven learning naturally reinforces academic skills while keeping kids excited and motivated.
Participants will learn how to use mystery fiction to:
Teach the scientific method through clue analysis, hypothesis building, and evidence evaluation
Explore forensic science, archaeology, geology, and environmental science through age-appropriate story scenarios
Introduce history and historical ethics, including artifact preservation, stolen art, secret societies, and outlaw legends
Strengthen logic, reasoning, and problem-solving skills without relying on worksheets
This session is designed with homeschool families in mind—offering scalable ideas for individual learners, family read-alouds, and co-op classes. Parents will leave with practical strategies and lesson plans they can use immediately to transform literature into rich, interdisciplinary learning experiences.
Mysteries don’t just entertain—they teach students how to think. When kids learn like detectives, curiosity becomes the curriculum.

title: Learning Really to Hear Good Music: A Fireside Chat
Learning to Read the Right Way - How to Teach Early Literacy Skills
description: Research emphasizes the importance of developing early reading foundational skills. Unfortunately, there are many children that fail to read proficiently due to lack of instruction in these early reading skills. They are often overlooked in hopes that children will "just get it" but can be detrimental later on. In this workshop I will show you exactly what early reading skills to introduce, how to introduce them, and at what age (starting at age 3). We'll discuss these essential skills and how mastery of them will lead to reading and spelling.

title: Let Me Tell You A Story
description: The ancients learned the art of storytelling as a form of learning. We are losing that art. Homeschoolers should excel in it.

title: Let's Get Writing!
description:
Writing is an art form achieved via a series of steps:
It all begins with an IDEA. Without an idea, the writer will simply stare at the blank page. Once there is an idea in the mind of the writer, the PENCIL steps in to translate thoughts to words on the page. When the pencil’s work is complete, the job of the writer is to become a READER. Encourage your students to RE-READ everything they write. Empower students to use the RED PEN as they re-read to REVISE. Teach them to use strong words, to fearlessly re-arrange, to make corrections, and to not be afraid to strike through. Polish the draft, preferably in cursive by hand. Join us for tips and tricks to get your students happily engaged in the process!

title: Life: The Greatest and Most Sacred Gift
description:
The workshop will NOT contain graphic imagery, nor it will have a judgmental tone. It will focus on the scientific development of a baby in her/his mother's womb, on the deep connection that baby and mother develop during the pregnancy, on the sacredness of every human life as it is created in the image of God, on the ways we can actively and compassionately help mothers and babies in need.
The workshop begins with a question to the audience: "If you could send a message to your unborn child (question for parents)/unborn baby sister or brother (question for kids), what would you say?" The audience will be given cards and pencils, so that they can write their answers on the cards. At the end of the workshop, I will invite the audience to paste all their cards on a poster I will provide.
The structure of the workshop is the following:
- Opening: The Zygote
I will project the image of a newly formed zygote and ask the following question: "What do you see in this image?" I will allow for individual reflection and initial reactions, and listen to the audience's answers. Then I will give my answer: “This is a person, a human being in her/his very first stage of life.”
- Section 1: The Miracle of Development – Science & Wonder
I will project slides, photos and short video clips to provide a visual timeline of fetal development, highlighting key milestones (e.g., heartbeat, brain waves, movement). The focus is to frame the baby's development as an “amazing engineering project” orchestrated by God.
- Section 2: The Mother-Baby Connection – Science & Scripture
I will provide scientific evidence (from experts in this field) of the the early formation of the mother-baby bond during pregnancy, then I will dive deeper in the Bible references that describe the project of infinite love that God has for each and every single one of His children, and how we are called to recognize each person (even the tiniest and voiceless one, like an unborn baby) as God's creation.
- Section 3: Living Out Respect for Life – Compassion & Action
I will ask the audience to participate in the "Walk in Her Shoes" activity: I will hand out simple scenarios depicting difficult situations a pregnant woman might face (e.g., unplanned pregnancy, financial hardship, relationship challenges). Each family will receive a set. The families will discuss each scenario, role-playing how they would respond with compassion and understanding and they will be encouraged to consider the emotional, spiritual, and practical needs of the mother. I will facilitate the discussion asking "What practical ways can we, as a community and as families, offer support to women facing challenging pregnancies?" (e.g., providing childcare, offering emotional support, connecting them with resources and organizations that will take care of them and their babies during and after pregnancy for as long as they need help).
- Conclusion: “Seeds of Action” – Small, Practical Steps
I will conclude the workshop giving a tiny bag of flower seeds to each family to bring home and to grow, together with a list of 3-4 simple ideas, small, concrete steps we can take to demonstrate our commitment to protecting and valuing human life.

title: Listening to Literature to Can Level Up Your Homeschool Classroom!
description: In a world where an 11 second reel rules, your family can break the cycle of short attention spans and dysregulation with the spoken word. Audio books and audio dramas can engage your student’s heart and mind to genuinely enjoy learning from words without images. They won’t need pictures because they will do their own world building in their minds! Learn how to incorporate listening as a learning tool in your homeschool!

title: Living Math: Teaching Off the Page in the Charlotte Mason Style
description: Math is often taught in isolation, disconnected from the world and the daily experiences of our kids. But math is meant to be taught in context, connected topic by topic to real-life problems and scenarios. Many of us know this but moving beyond workbooks feels daunting and mysterious. Exactly how are we supposed to bring math to life? Kathryn will show you how to invigorate your math program in 3 steps during this Charlotte Mason-inspired math session. You’ll walk away with fresh vision and practical tips that you can start using right away.

title: Made in God’s Image: Recovering a Biblical Imagination for Our Children
description: In a culture saturated with distorted narratives, this talk calls parents and educators to reorient the imaginations of children toward the truth of being made in God’s image. By recovering a biblical vision of identity, beauty, and purpose, we equip the next generation to see the world—and themselves—through the lens of Scripture. Rooted in Biblical theology, this talk inspires Christ-centered formation through art, storytelling, and faithful cultural engagement.

title: Made Male and Female: Teaching art rooted in the truth of our gender
description: This talk explores how Christian parents and educators can teach art in a way that affirms God’s good design of male and female. Rather than flattening differences or feeding cultural confusion, we’ll discuss how to honor the distinct strengths, perspectives, and creative impulses of boys and girls. Rooted in Scripture and beauty, this talk encourages raising young makers to glorify God through art that reflects the truth of who they are.

title: Magically Delicious: Breakfast Cereal Spokespersons and Media Influence
description:
Magically Delicious: Breakfast Cereal Spokespersons and Media Influence
We as parents know that popular breakfast cereals are generally loaded with sugar, among other ingredients that may not be exactly healthy for us. Yet, we eat these cereals and also let our children eat them, anyway! Why? In this session, we’ll take a fun tour through the history of cereal advertising as an illustration of the power of media to influence the thinking and choices of kids of all ages.

title: Martin and Andrew Reflect on Great Children’s Literature
description: Join Andrew Pudewa and Martin Cothran for a delightful romp through fifteen of the best Children’s books for young, middle, and older students (or yourself!).

title: Martin and Andrew Reflect on Great Children’s Literature
description: Join Andrew Pudewa and Martin Cothran for a delightful romp through fifteen of the best Children’s books for young, middle, and older students (or yourself!).

title: Multi-Age, Multi-Grade? All Together without Mom Losing her Mind!
description: Are you longing to find ways in your day to bring all your children together around the table? Do you need refreshing tips that show you how to combine subjects in a way that meets the needs of all your children – on their levels and in their styles of learning? Are you wondering which subjects you can do together? Are you weary of juggling different grade levels? Spend some time with Julia Nalle as she shares numerous practical helps for teaching history, literature, writing, Bible, geography, art, science and more across multi‐age levels drawing from both a classical style and a Charlotte Mason approach.

title: Music for Every Mind: Teaching All Learners, All Abilities
description: Music has the power to reach every child—regardless of age, ability, or learning style. In this uplifting and practical session, Adrian Edward, founder of Color Me Mozart®, shares how music can become a joyful and accessible part of your homeschool, especially for children with special needs. Whether your child is neurodivergent, nonverbal, or simply learns differently, you’ll walk away with easy-to-use tools and encouraging insights to bring the gift of music into their world—no musical background required.

title: My Child Wants to Make Movies… What Do I Do?!
description:
When your child first expresses interest in making movies or even pursuing it as a career…it may seem a bit overwhelming! What kind of equipment do they need to get started? Should they consider film school or are there other educational opportunities? Is this even a viable career path?
Craig Dehut, documentary filmmaker, media consultant, and Co-Founder/CEO of Appian Media (www.appianmedia.org) shares his own experiences of graduating from film school, learning alongside industry professionals, and pursuing a rather “non-linear” path toward a full-time career in video - while providing many alternative educational options, inexpensive equipment suggestions, and resources for you to help your child develop their interest in video production!

title: Navigating Dual Enrollment: From a Homeschool Mom to a College Insider
description: Considering dual enrollment but unsure where to start? Join Alison Agnew, a homeschool mom who's been in your shoes, for a practical guide to the process. Drawing from her own successful experience with four children and her current work in higher education, she offers the wisdom of both a parent and a professional. This workshop will help you identify the best options for your child and equip you with the knowledge to make smart decisions and confidently plan their path to college.

title: Navigating the Shift: A Supportive Guide to Transitioning from Public School to Home Education
description: Many parents are making the shift to homeschooling after their children have experienced traditional school settings. While this transition can be incredibly rewarding, it also comes with significant adjustments. How do you break away from ingrained school-based routines and reframe your approach to education, all while teaching your child at home? What are the common pitfalls to avoid, and which deeply held educational paradigms should be reexamined? In this session, you’ll gain the encouragement and insights needed to embrace a new mindset, overcome challenges, and confidently navigate this transition to homeschooling.

title: New Homeschoolers Wanted: Discover the Roadmap for Homeschooling Success!
description: Designed to give you confidence as you begin your homeschooling journey. In this session, you’ll learn the key steps that set families up for long-term success—from choosing curriculum with confidence, to creating a rhythm that works for your home, to avoiding the most common mistakes that overwhelm new homeschoolers. You’ll leave with a clear roadmap, practical strategies, and the encouragement you need to start strong and enjoy the journey. Taught by a second-generation homeschooling mom

title: Obliterate the Federal Department of Education: the Framers' Solution
description:
It was not an oversight that the framers left education out of the US Constitution and the Bill of Rights. Education was never even mentioned in our founding documents. The omission of education clearly implies that the states have authority over education, and are not beholden to educational concepts, policies, and political philosophies of the federal government. The 10th Amendment clarifies that education should be regulated at the state level:
The powers not delegated to the United States by the Constitution, nor prohibited by it to the States, are reserved to the States respectively, or to The People.
It is up to We the People to take back control over education at a local level. Steve Jones from Convention of States will discuss the use of Article V to bring education solely into the control of the states and The People.

title: Online Courses: How to Choose and Be Successful While Figuring Out What Courses I Can Or Can’t Teach Myself.
description: Online courses are becoming more and more prevalent in the Homeschool arena. This talk will assist you in determining which courses you may feel comfortable and competent teaching yourself, how to choose an online course and even more important what to look for in an instructor. Online courses are a major investment and can be a valuable resource for your child’s overall success at home.

title: Organizing the Disorganized: Practical Tools for Kids Who Can’t Find Their Shoes
description: Does your child lose track of assignments, misplace belongings, or struggle to follow multi-step directions? You’re not alone—and there are solutions! This practical, parent-focused workshop provides strategies for building working memory, planning, and organization skills in children and teens. From creating checklists and visual zones to using labels and digital supports, you’ll walk away with simple, concrete ideas you can put into practice right away. Whether your child is neurodivergent or just “scattered,” this session will equip you with tools to reduce frustration, foster independence, and help your child feel more capable and confident.

title: Origins & Worldview: Thinking Critically About Creation and Evolution
description: Is the creation/evolution debate really about science versus faith—or is it a clash between two competing histories? This workshop helps homeschooling parents understand how worldview shapes the interpretation of scientific evidence. Learn how biblical history and Darwinian history offer different lenses for understanding the world, and discover practical ways to teach your children to evaluate scientific claims using internal critique.

title: Parenting Tech Safe Kids: from Toddlers to Teens
description: Up to 75% of kids see inappropriate content by age 17. This session equips you with an empowered approach, utilizing developmentally grounded strategies, for raising tech-wise kids. Learn how to foster healthy screen habits, open conversations about online risks, and recognize the surprising ways kids access the internet and chat rooms. You’ll leave with practical resources, including steps for responding to online exploitation.

title: Parenting the Incredible Child: Transforming Strong Emotions into a Super Power
description: When our child has strong emotions of anger, sadness, or excitement, we can feel too weak to help them cope. In this session, you’ll be empowered with knowledge, empathy, and the tools you need to guide your incredible child with confidence. This session is suitable for parents of neurodivergent, gifted, and otherwise emotionally responsive kids and teens.

title: Planting Word Seeds: Nurturing a Love of Language Through Read-Alouds
description: Help your children fall in love with words! In this practical and encouraging session, you’ll discover simple ways to build strong vocabularies through meaningful read-aloud time and playful, low-prep activities. You’ll leave with fresh ideas and easy strategies to make vocabulary growth a joyful part of your homeschool days. Participants will take home a vocabulary-builder poster to use with read-alouds.

title: Play to Learn: Transforming Curriculum with Games
description: Discover how game-based learning can transform your homeschool experience! This session explores the benefits, strategies, and practical steps for integrating educational games into your curriculum. Drawing on research and real-world examples, including the GET 9 math game, we’ll discuss how games can enhance student engagement, support diverse learning styles, and foster critical thinking, collaboration, and social-emotional growth.

title: Poetry and Play
speakerName: Rachel S. Donahue & Carolyn Clare Givens
description:
What if adults played with words in order to provide children words to play with? The upcoming children's poetry anthology from Bandersnatch Books was inspired by this very question. Engaging children with poetry is best done by playing with words, and that play can become a great joy for all involved.

How do you develop a delight in rhymes, puns, and other wordplay in your children? How do you approach selecting poetry for children to dive into? What skills does a love of poetry develop in young readers?

Poet and editor Rachel S. Donahue and writer and publisher Carolyn Clare Givens will discuss these questions and more, along with sharing recommendations of poetry to include in your daily wordplay.

title: Preparing Families for Online Learning
description:
As homeschooling families increasingly incorporate online learning into their routines, many are asking: Are our children truly prepared for the digital classroom?
This session will explore practical strategies to help families create a supportive and effective home environment for online learning. Drawing on real experiences and common challenges, such as screen fatigue, motivation dips, and tech frustrations, we’ll identify ways to reduce stress and improve readiness. Attendees will leave with actionable tips to better equip their children and households for success in virtual learning.
Designed with homeschoolers in mind, this session offers a blend of empathy, insight, and hands-on advice to help families feel more confident navigating online education.

title: Preparing For (and Parenting) the Teen Years
Prime Numbers - The Gateway to Algebra and Beyond!
description: This hands-on workshop will show how to use prime numbers to simplify both numerical and algebraic fractions, greatest common factors, least common multiples, simplifying roots and determining patterns in sequences. This workshop is for anyone who is ready for pre-algebra and up to pre-calculus. The workshop is fast paced and fun whether you are a middle school student, high school student or parent. This is a must for parents of younger children preparing for pre-algebra!

title: Prime Numbers – The Gateway to Algebra and Beyond!
description: This hands-on workshop will show how to use prime numbers to simplify both numerical and algebraic fractions, greatest common factors, least common multiples, simplifying roots and determining patterns in sequences. This workshop is for anyone who is ready for pre-algebra and up to pre-calculus. The workshop is fast paced and fun whether you are a middle school student, high school student or parent. This is a must for parents of younger children preparing for pre-algebra!

title: Promise Keys – Helping Your Child Know God Experientially for Themselves
description:
Children of all ages need to know God experientially for themselves. They need to know His character, how He moves, how to engage Him, hear Him, and receive from Him so that they have their own personal relationship with Him.

Promise Keys is a fully illustrated and interactive children’s discipleship curriculum that is designed to help children know God intimately and personally. It includes Bible stories, object lessons, Holy Spirit activation encounters, illustrated characters, memory verses, biblical blessings, games, art, movement, various creative activities, and teacher notes to provide all you need to introduce children to the person of God, not the “system” of God.

This workshop will introduce you to the concept and methods of Promise Keys and empower you to steward your children into deeper understanding and encounter with the Lord while having fun, moving their bodies, and being creative. Come see what is possible and discover God's heart to reveal Himself to your children!

title: Raining on Your History Parade
description: Were you trained to think that history is just a parade of civilizations, events, heroes and villains? If so, I’m about to rain on your parade. History is so much more than that. History is a long conversation about the ideas that shaped those civilizations, events, heroes, and villains and how they fit into God’s story. History is about exploring the truth, evaluating human nature, and making connections. Learning to help your children trace the ideas that have shaped history will enable them to understand where we’ve been and how and why we are where we are today.

title: Raising Christ-Centered Children
description: As parents, our responsibility to raise our children begins and ends with Christ. We want to introduce our children to Christ, but more so, we want to introduce our children to the character of Christ. In this workshop, we will discuss successful, Biblical methods to fulfill this responsibility.

title: Raising Courageous Boys: A Roadmap to Biblical Manhood
description:
Raising Courageous Boys: A Roadmap to Biblical Manhood
In a world that’s confused about masculinity, how can we guide our sons toward godly manhood? In this session, author, podcaster, and mom of four boys Laurie Christine will provide a roadmap for raising boys into strong, courageous men of faith. Learn how boys are uniquely wired by God, how to disciple them with intentionality, and how to create meaningful rites of passage that affirm their identity in Christ. You’ll leave with practical tools, inspiration, and a vision for helping your sons become warriors for God’s Kingdom.

title: Raising Kids Who Don't Hate You
Raising Lions, Not Labradors
description:
God’s Vision for Masculinity
The world doesn’t need easier boys.

It needs better men.
Somewhere along the way, strength became something to manage instead of something to form. Courage became “too much.” Leadership became “problematic.” And parents were told — subtly, constantly — to domesticate what God designed to be directed.
This talk reframes the conversation entirely.

Raising Lions, Not Labradors is not about making boys aggressive or loud. It’s about refusing to shrink them. It’s about shaping strength with truth, anchoring confidence in Christ, and forming sons who know when to stand, when to serve, and when to lead.

If you’ve ever wondered whether raising a strong son is still allowed — this talk answers that question clearly.
It is. And it matters.

title: Raising Lions, Not Lambs: How God & Guns Inspire Courage in Our Kids
description: Today’s culture is discipling our children to fear—fear responsibility, fear adulthood, fear speaking truth, and especially fear the very rights God has given to preserve life and liberty. In this session, Erich Pratt unpacks the biblical foundations of self-defense—from Exodus 22:2–3 to Jesus’ command in Luke 22:36 to Israel’s disarmament crisis in 1 Samuel 13—and shows why Christian parents must intentionally teach their children the true history and purpose of the Second Amendment. When kids aren’t grounded in these truths, they become more vulnerable to fear-based narratives that undermine courage, duty, and moral clarity. But when we train them with Scripture and the principles of a free people, we raise young men and women who can stand boldly, think critically, and lead with conviction in a world that desperately needs it.

title: Raising Mission Minded Children in a Me Centered World
description: Practical ways you can instill in your children a heart for missions and the desire to serve others in Christ's name for a life-time.

title: Raising Patriots: Teach Your Children to Love America
description: How do you teach your children to cherish America’s unique heritage? God has showered bounties on the American people. We are blessed with freedoms and prosperity, built on Christian values, that are historically without precedent. America is not perfect but we have a rich heritage of striving to live up to the ideals of our founding. Yet our culture, from our schools to our media, surrounds our children with stories that make them ashamed to be Americans. Discover practical ways to raise the next generation of patriots who embrace civic duty and celebrate America’s blessings!

title: Raising Self-Sufficient Kids Through Food Science
description: Food Science is the ultimate every day useful science. It improves cooking and baking skills, allows you to troubleshoot in the kitchen, save money by avoiding wasting ingredients, and lets you have fun by giving you the knowledge you need to innovate with confidence. Food Science can even become a career! Come and listen to a professional food scientist talk about how his hands-on experiment-based courses can help your students, from kindergarten all the way through high school, become more confident and self-sufficient. Dale will share anecdotes of his time working in the food industry you will find not only entertaining, but educational as well. With plenty of time for Q&A, this will be an hour you won’t forget. These courses offer features for special needs students as well. Beakers & Bricks, LLC’s goal is to only teach at least practical science that will improve your life. Food science meets that goal in abundance…come see why!

title: Raising Self-Sufficient Kids Through Practical Physics
description: While Dale is a professional food scientist, he is also a lover of all things LEGO® and physics. Ever since discovering that physics describes how most everything works around us Dale has been fascinated by the science. Practical physics, the kind you actually use around the house, is especially helpful improving the ability of your students to become confident and useful adults. The goal is to learn the physics and math principles behind common household machines, then apply those principles to make and build machines themselves, using the Technic® type of LEGO® building elements. These courses offer features for special needs students as well. With offerings for young and middle school/high school aged students, you will find something that works for your family. Come join us for a great hour talking about physics and how it can help you and your children improve their lives!

title: Raising Your Princesses and Knights for the Kingdom
description: We all desire kids who are filled with character, making good choices and grow up to be world changers. How do we get there? Experience has given me a roadmap that we can all relate to and learn from. I will share all the bumps I had along the way to encourage us!

title: Reader Response Journals and Your Homeschool Classroom!
description: Inspire your child to love reading and writing! Learn how to use Reader Response Journals in your homeschool. Help your learner find meaning and genuinely connect to the lessons waiting for them in your favorite books. This method of studying literature is for any age and any reading level. Leave with a plan in place and sample book lists for elementary, middle, and high school. You CAN create your own curriculum for your children with the books you love.

title: Reading Problem? Let’s Fix It!
description: Is your child experiencing reading difficulties? This workshop uses a question/answer format to tailor the content for your child's specific needs. After 42 years of experience, I have seen and fixed almost every reading problem imaginable. Come prepared with your questions. I want to help your child become a successful reader.

title: Reading Together: Unlocking the Power of Read-Aloud for Struggling Readers
description: When your child finds independent reading challenging, it can feel hard to know how to help. Have you considered the power of reading aloud together? In this workshop, we’ll explore how a rich read-aloud life can become one of your most valuable tools. You’ll discover practical ways to build on your child’s strengths, nurture a genuine love for stories, and gently strengthen their reading skills—one shared book at a time.

title: Real Latin Means Real Advantages
description: There are a zillion ways to dabble and play with homeschool Latin, but getting serious about it offers major benefits for your middle and high school student. How do you prepare them? What qualifies as real Latin? Why not teach them a modern language? Find out what Latin can do for you.

title: Reclaiming the Mother-Healer: How Your Peace Becomes Your Children's First Health Lesson
description: We are raising our children in a world that constantly tells mothers to doubt themselves. Everywhere you look—online, in doctor’s offices, in conversations with well-meaning friends—moms are quietly encouraged to silence their own intuition and trade it for fear, dependence, and medical outsourcing. But what if God designed mothers with something deeper? What if He gave you instincts, discernment, and inner wisdom specifically for the purpose of caring for your children—not just academically, not just spiritually, but physically as well? In this transformational workshop, Paola invites mothers to reclaim what they’ve lost: their confidence. Their inner knowing. Their ability to observe illness without fear. Their God-given capacity to nurture and lead their family’s health with courage and clarity. Drawing from her own story and her powerful Mindset Reset teachings, Paola equips mothers to release guilt from past decisions, quiet the noise of modern medicine, and hear the still, small voice that guides us in moments of uncertainty. As parents, we must not only offer our children food and shelter—we must model what it looks like to consciously and prayerfully steward our own bodies. Today’s world is obsessed with the “pill for every ill” mentality, and many mothers feel overwhelmed or discouraged trying to navigate health choices for their kids. But when you learn how to think critically, listen spiritually, and trust yourself, everything changes.

title: Resources For Supplementing Your Child’s Education
description:
Picking the right educational platform is a big decision! At Liberty University Online Academy, we want to partner with you as you make that choice. Our program is flexible and designed to work for all families. Come discover our full-time, supplemental, and dual enrollment programs that are ready to help you and your child grow academically. LUOA is the best of homeschool, private school, and Bible-based education all rolled into one!

12q

title: Rethink High School- Learn Smarter, Build Life & Career Skills
description: It’s time to rethink high school so your student can focus on a strategized and purposeful education that allows time for career exploration, leadership development, life skills, extracurricular activities, volunteering, and internships. Students replace long lectures and endless busywork with short, mastery-based lessons while building critical thinking skills, academic confidence, and independence. Imagine your teen enjoying high school, finding their strengths, and being prepared for success after graduation.

title: Rethink Robotics Education: Teach Robotics in your home for free
description:
Most parents assume robotics requires expensive kits, complicated setups, or advanced engineering knowledge — but modern robotics education looks very different. In this workshop, we’ll show you how to bring meaningful, high-engagement robotics learning into your homeschool without purchasing hardware or equipment.
Many homeschool parents ask:
“How can we teach robotics if we can’t afford the materials?”
“Is robotics only possible with physical robots?”
“Where do we even start?”
This session rethinks the entire concept of robotics education for homeschool families. We’ll explore approachable, budget-friendly ways to teach real robotics concepts using digital tools, simulations, and interactive challenges that require little to no upfront cost.
Parents will see firsthand how robotics education can be accessible, exciting, and affordable — and how students can gain the same skills taught in traditional robotics programs using tools already available to them.
Whether your child has never touched a robot or is ready for advanced challenges, this workshop will give you a fresh perspective on what robotics education can look like and how to start today at no cost.

title: Rethinking Failure: How Mistakes Make Masters
description: Most students fear failure—but what if failure was the best teacher of all? In this eye-opening workshop, We help students and parents reframe mistakes as stepping stones to mastery. Using stories, research, and interactive activities, attendees will learn how to turn setbacks into strategies, practice resilience, and embrace the growth mindset. Parents will discover how to coach their children through frustration without pressure, and students will leave ready to see mistakes as their best learning tool.

title: Rethinking the Strong-Willed Child: Charlotte Mason’s Powerful Insights
description: If you're exhausted from raising a strong-willed child, you've heard the reassuring words: “She'll resist peer pressure! He's a born leader!” These ideas offer comfort—but what if they prove false? Charlotte Mason understood the defiant child doesn't have a strong will at all, but a weak one, dominated by emotions and impulses. As a mom of five sons, some who “qualified” as “strong-willed,” this insight changed everything. Discover the truth popular teaching has gotten wrong and come away with hope and practical methods aligned with Scripture.

title: Revolutionary Insights: Bridging the Gap Between Reading and Spelling Mastery
description:
Does your excellent reader struggle with spelling, leaving you wondering if their difficulties are normal?
Discover how neuroscience illuminates the profound connection between reading and spelling, revealing
why conventional methods often fall short. Join Gretchen Roe to explore a more effective approach that
fosters seamless, lifelong literacy skills for your student, regardless of past struggles.

title: SAT Math – It’s Not the Math, It’s the Question that is Confusing!
description:
Join a mini-SAT Boot Camp session. Learn the Mr. D secrets of test taking tips and techniques from his SAT Boot Camp. This workshop will focus on math, the language behind the questions and show participants what they really need to know before taking the math section of the SAT. Find out the words on the test that give tips for how to solve the questions as well learning what formulas you really need to know before the taking the SAT. Students and parents alike
will learn how to unravel the questions being asked into something they can solve quickly and easily. These techniques can be used for other testing situations and subject areas as well.

title: SAT Math – It’s Not the Math, It’s the Question that is Confusing!
description:
Join a mini-SAT Boot Camp session. Learn the Mr. D secrets of test taking tips and techniques from his SAT Boot Camp. This workshop will focus on math, the language behind the questions and show participants what they really need to know before taking the math section of the SAT. Find out the words on the test that give tips for how to solve the questions as well learning what formulas you really need to know before the taking the SAT. Students and parents alike
will learn how to unravel the questions being asked into something they can solve quickly and easily. These techniques can be used for other testing situations and subject areas as well.

title: Science and Christianity: Partners in the Search for Truth
description: While many people have the mistaken idea that science and Christianity are “at war” with one another, the history of science tells us a completely different story. In this talk, Dr. Jay L. Wile shows the profound influence that Christianity had on the development of science. By discussing specific historical events (such as the development of the scientific method) and specific scientific advances (like Kepler’s Laws), he shows that without Christianity, we would not have the science that we have today.

title: Science in the Kitchen, Dinner on the Table
description: Are you searching for hands-on science activities with practical applications? Look no further than your kitchen! In this workshop, we’ll explore the role of science in cooking and discuss how to approach the subject in a way that leads to meaningful, delicious results.

title: Science of Reading VS Sight Word Memorization:Reading & Spelling Can Be Fun
description: This session will show you why one method leaves kids frustrated while the other unlocks lasting success. You’ll discover how playful, science-backed strategies can turn reading and spelling from a daily struggle into something your kids actually enjoy. Come ready to rethink what you thought you knew—and leave with tools that make learning click.

title: Scientific Discoveries that Have Been Made as a Result the Christian Faith
description: While many people have the mistaken idea that faith cannot be incorporated into scientific research, the fact is that some of the most important scientific discoveries were the direct result of the scientist incorporating his or her Christian faith into his or her research program. In this talk, Dr. Jay L. Wile, a nuclear chemist, discusses scientists from ancient times to the present who have used their Christian faith as a guide to make incredible scientific discoveries.

title: Sculpting Your Child's Worldview With Literature
description: Most homeschoolers have a faith foundation. All have a moral foundation. Does the cultural content you bring into your home align with both? In this workshop, publisher and teacher Kiri Jorgensen will show you how to build realistic alliances between children’s literature and faith. She’ll give you specific examples and sculpting tools to help as you guide your children to shape their own worldviews.

title: Sharia Law vs. the Constitution: A War of Principles
description: In America, most people have little understanding of the harsh realities within Sharia Law. Its core principles simply do not align with the American constitutional system or our way of life. Let’s look inside the structure, practices, and governing beliefs of Sharia. Learn why it stands in direct conflict with the freedoms we value—and why it’s essential that we push back against any attempt to normalize it. Massey will also equip you with practical ways to do exactly that.

title: Shattering the Illusion : The Real Dangers of Pornography
description: In a world where digital content is only a click away, pornography has become one of the most common—and least understood—influences on young minds. This eye-opening workshop helps homeschooling parents navigate this challenging topic with confidence, clarity, and compassion. Together, we’ll explore how pornography impacts brain development, emotions, relationships, and self-image, and why early conversations matter more than ever. Parents will gain practical tools for fostering open dialogue, setting healthy digital boundaries, and guiding their children toward genuine connection, self-respect, and a healthy understanding of love in a hyperconnected world

title: Shift From Perfectionist to High Achiever: Your Fresh Start!
description: Do you ever feel like no matter how much you plan, prep, or push, your homeschool still doesn’t look the way you imagined? Perfectionism has a sneaky way of draining the joy from our homeschool days—keeping us stuck and weighed down by regret. In this transformative workshop, you’ll uncover five practical shifts to help you think like a high achiever—so you can finally break free from perfectionism and feel aligned with the kind of mom, teacher, and leader you truly want to be. You’ll leave with simple tools, fresh clarity, and a renewed vision for your homeschool life. This is your fresh start.

title: Simplifying the Photo, Video, Kids' Art & Keepsake Overload
description: Do you have old films, VHS tapes, slides that you can not play because you don't have the equipment? Gail will share things to consider when digitizing old media. Do it yourself, Gail will explain how to do digitization and give helpful pointers for the "Do It Yourselfer." Points to consider for sending out your old media and the importance of redundancy.

title: Simplifying the Photo, Video, Kids' Art & Keepsake Overload
description: Overloaded with your, your parents, or your grandparents boxes of photos, outdated media, and keepsakes? How about your own digital photos, homeschool memories, and stories? In this workshop, you'll learn five simple steps to successfully do it yourself or use it as a homeschool learning module! Your kids and family will be enriched by bringing these photos and stories into their lives!

title: So You Want to Be a Novelist
description: Storytelling Techniques that go Beyond the Basics You have an amazing story idea, and you want to write a great novel. Now, after learning the basics of storytelling, you’re searching for how to step up to the next level. This is the class for you. Bestselling author Bryan Davis will teach several techniques that will enhance your storytelling skills, such as motivation/reaction units, conflict cycles, and intensity prods. These lessons are perfect for any plot-driven genre, and they can be modified for character-driven stories as well.

title: Sovereign Education: First Principles Needed in Every Curriculum
description: Equip your homeschool with timeless principles that build critical thinkers, courageous leaders, and creative problem-solvers … independent of state systems or trends. In an age of AI and conformity, our children need first principles more than ever. Scott unpacks how to teach adaptability over compliance, character over obedience, and confidence over dependence. These are skills that prepare kids for a rapidly changing world. Learn how “sovereign education” empowers your family to self-custody truth, think critically, and lead with conviction.

title: Speak Up, Stand Out: Speech and Debate Skills for Lifelong Success
description: Speak Up, Stand Out is an encouraging introduction for parents who want to equip their children with strong communication, critical thinking, and leadership skills through speech and debate. Focusing on the American Speech and Debate Association (ASDA), this workshop will show you how to get your family involved in values-based, family-friendly tournaments. Whether your child is shy or outspoken, you'll learn how speech and debate can help them grow in confidence, think clearly, and speak with impact --both in competition and in life.

title: Stack Up for Success: Building Skills Through Movement and Teamwork
description: Discover how Sport Stacking can bring energy, focus, and fun into your homeschool routine! In this interactive session, you’ll learn simple ways to use Speed Stacks cups as a tool for movement breaks, family challenges, and even academic connections. Sport Stacking isn’t just about cups—it builds hand-eye coordination, critical thinking, and confidence while giving kids an outlet for healthy activity. Whether you’re looking for new ways to keep your students moving, or an activity the whole family can enjoy together, this session will leave you with practical games, ideas, and resources to bring home.

title: Stop Apologizing for Your Son
description:
Raising Boys the World Keeps Trying to Soften
You don’t need a better explanation.

You need a better conviction.
Parents today are exhausted from defending normal boyhood — energy, competitiveness, boldness — as if it’s a flaw. The quiet message is constant: tone him down, make him safer, smooth the edges. This talk calls that out.

Stop Apologizing for Your Son is a line in the sand for parents who are done asking permission to raise boys with backbone. It exposes how cultural fear has replaced formation, why softness is being confused with virtue, and how apologizing for your son slowly teaches him to apologize for himself.

This isn’t a talk about rebellion.
It’s about resolve.
Because boys don’t need to be softened.

title: Strategies for Teaching Children and Adults to Love Art
Stress-Free College Application—Is It Possible?
description: The college application process is frequently associated with stress and uncertainty, yet with thoughtful preparation it can be both manageable and meaningful. This session will examine practical approaches to reducing application-related anxiety, including strategies for maintaining accurate academic and extracurricular records throughout high school, understanding and contextualizing standardized test scores, and identifying institutions that reflect a student’s values and future goals. In addition, the workshop will explore how dual enrollment opportunities can distinguish applicants while providing an early academic advantage. Participants will gain tools to support students in navigating the application process with confidence and clarity.

title: Structure Without Stress: How to Plan Your Year with Flexibility
description: Homeschool planning often feels like dreaming big and then crashing into reality. This workshop will help you get real about what you can actually accomplish in a year—without guilt or overwhelm. Learn how to set realistic goals, build a flexible framework, and focus on steady progress instead of chasing perfection. We’ll explore how to create rhythms that work for your family, adjust plans when life throws curveballs, and still finish the year with a sense of accomplishment. Walk away with tools to plan confidently, flex when needed, and homeschool with peace of mind.

title: Struggling Learners: Getting to the Root
description:
SMART CHILD, STRUGGLING LEARNER.

Does Your Smart Child Struggle with Learning? Does he/she:
• Reverse Letters?
• Show poor body awareness & gets into other's space?
• Can't remember what has been taught from day to day?
• Has a diagnosis of ADHD, Dyslexia or Dysgraphia?
• Appear slow & slightly "vacant" when spoken to?
• Have difficulty following multiple instructions?
• Have a short attention span?
• Show poor balance?
• Behave immaturely for their age?
• Withdraw/avoid large crowds?

This workshop will show you what might be the root cause and the drug-free solution that addresses it.

title: Take Your Photography Up a Level
description: Learn to be intentional with your photography. We all take pictures, but we don't always consider leading lines, composition, story, and subject. In this workshop learn from a pro how to take your photography to the next level whether you are using a phone or a $4000 camera.

title: Taking Teens’ Social Skills from Awkward to
description: How can we help our homeschooled teens develop excellent relationship skills without embarrassing them? We know that ignoring, lecturing, and hovering aren’t effective strategies. If you feel awkward addressing your teen’s social skills, you’ll feel awesome after learning practical, painless ways of helping your teen make friends, participate in a group, and resolve conflict.

title: Taking your Mom Mind Captive
description: From my cancer journeys to homeschooling, the words swimming in my head have led me down negative paths. What do we do? Thoughts have the power to build up or tear down our mind and then spew ugly words to those around us, usually our family. Our inner words or thoughts, can create confidence or chaos for us. And they can be awkward to tell others! Through my own stories of failure and success, explore how we all can be successful in capturing the best God has for us - grabbing those unruly thoughts that start the God's truth!

title: Teach a Foreign Language--Even If You Don't Speak One
description: Think you need to be fluent to teach your kids another language? Think again! In this eye-opening workshop, you’ll discover how to become a Fluency Family—using a foreign language in your real, everyday homeschool life from day one. You’ll learn what fluency actually is (and what it’s not), how to avoid the 3 biggest mistakes that keep families stuck, and the 8 pitfalls that quietly sabotage your child’s motivation. Whether you’re teaching preschoolers, teens, or everything in between, you’ll leave with the confidence and tools to finally move beyond flashcards and grammar drills—and start using a foreign language with your family every single day.

title: Teach Music at Home with Confidence—No Experience Required
description: Ready to bring music into your homeschool with ease and confidence? In this fun, interactive session, we’ll show you how to give your child a strong foundation in music—no prior experience needed! Learn how to teach rhythm, melody, and music basics using simple, hands-on games and activities, even if you don’t play an instrument yourself. You’ll walk away with practical, ready-to-use techniques that will help spark your child’s love for music and make learning feel as natural as singing the ABCs. This session is perfect for homeschool parents who want to make music a fun, creative part of their child’s day, all while building confidence and creativity along the way!

title: Teach Your Child to Read the Fun, Stress-Free Way with ABC See, Hear, Do
description: Learning to read doesn’t have to be stressful. It can be fun! In this workshop, Stefanie Hohl, M.Ed., creator of ABC See, Hear, Do, will show you how to use movement and engaging activities to help your child master letter sounds and early reading skills. Whether your child is wiggly, easily frustrated, or just getting started, you’ll leave with practical strategies you can use right away to make reading click.

title: Teaching Freedom: How Financial Literacy Shapes Character
description: Discover how understanding free markets, not budgets or balance sheets, teaches kids the moral foundation of freedom, responsibility, and truth. Most money lessons focus on counting dollars instead of understanding principles. Scott Lindberg reveals how true financial literacy starts with free-market truth, why endless money printing erodes values, and how technology’s natural deflation shows God’s design for abundance. You’ll leave with simple stories and practical examples you can use to build both financial confidence and moral character in your homeschool.

title: Teaching Life Skills Without Overwhelm: A Simple Toolbox for Homeschool Parents
description: Homeschool parents know life skills matter, but knowing what to teach and how to teach it can feel overwhelming, especially when chores stall out or parents carry all the mental load. In this practical, how-to session, a veteran homeschooler and educator with over 20 years of experience will share six specific tools for turning everyday responsibilities into intentional life-skills training: ownership, progression, feedback, transfer, independence, and structure. You'll learn exactly how to give kids real ownership, help skills grow with age, provide meaningful feedback, and hand off responsibility without burning out. This session focuses on concrete strategies you can use immediately to build real-world readiness right into daily homeschool life, even into academic classes. Students are welcome to attend this session.

title: Teaching the Missions field at Home
description: Come learn how a 13-week unit study can open up your family to the world in which we live. Where more than 90% of all languages on this planet do not have a Whole Bible to read. We are all called to Give. Go. Pray. Do. Learn how to apply Missions to your family life and where you can be an impact for Christ.

title: Teens Fiction Editing Workshop
description:
“How do I know my if writing is any good? How can I improve it if it’s not?” If you’re a teen writer with these questions, then this workshop is for you! Together we’ll talk through what separates good writing from bad writing, edit a scene live on screen, and learn principles to level up your storytelling skills.
This presentation is aimed at teens of all ages who love to write, and parents who want to learn how to provide feedback on their stories outside of spelling and grammar.

title: Test Prep: Higher Scores = Dream School and Less Debt
description: Want your child to get accepted to their dream school? Are you worried about how you will pay for that dream? High scores can help. Discover proven strategies to maximize performance on the ACT, SAT and CLT. Learn how higher test scores can expand scholarship opportunities, strengthen college applications and increase financial aid awards. Parents and students will leave with a clear strategic timeline and the knowledge of how test prep is a key component to a successful college acceptance process.

title: The ‘Funnest’ Party House on The Block
description:
(Make your home the go-to place for all your friends and kids’ friends.)
There are seven secrets to throwing the best and most memorable party in town; SPOILER ALERT —it’s not the food or decorations that make the party. Jean Burk is known as the “party queen” in Texas for very good reason and she wants to share her best secrets. She’s hosted and planned over 100 successful, fun and memorable parties. (adult, teen, birthday, bridal, theme...)
Learn exactly how Jean does it. She’ll show you how to set the right party tone to create immediate excitement for guests and ultimately generate 100% attendance. Your parties will be the talk of the town for weeks

title: The 7 Keys for Choosing Curriculum
description: Learn the 7 keys for choosing curriculum that promotes academic excellence and breathes life and joy into your household.

title: The AI-Ready Family: Being Prepared For the Most Powerful Tech
description:
The AI-Ready Family: Being Prepared For (and Protected From) the Most Powerful Tech We’ve Ever Conceived
Join us for a dynamic, eye-opening presentation designed to equip families with the knowledge and practical strategies needed to navigate the rapidly advancing world of artificial intelligence.
In this session, attendees will discover how AI is shaping everyday life, from education, productivity, and creativity to privacy, safety, and digital well-being. The presentation breaks down complex concepts into clear, actionable guidance that empowers parents, teens, and children to use AI responsibly and confidently. Participants will learn how to identify risks, protect personal data, and establish healthy digital habits at home, while also exploring inspiring ways AI can support learning, careers, and innovation. This session emphasizes empowerment over fear, offering families a balanced roadmap to thrive in a future where artificial intelligence is both a powerful tool and a responsibility.
In this session, you’ll learn:
The three functional areas of the human brain.
Why AI-fueled cognitive offloading is especially detrimental during adolescence.
Why 72% of teens are already using AI companions (and what that means for your family).
Why every home needs 5 layers of protection in the AI Age.
This is not just theory—we’re bringing you real-world solutions, smart strategies, and tips you can start using right away. Join us and take a big step toward a more confident, connected, and AI-ready family life (Q&A at the end).

title: The Art of Raising an Artist
description: How can parents spot their child’s artistic talent early, nurture creativity at home, and guide them toward a successful art career? Davy Liu—former Academy of Art University faculty and professional filmmaker with Disney, Warner Bros., and Lucasfilm—shares expert insights on building portfolios, choosing the right schools, and navigating today’s art industry.

title: The Beauty of Classical Education
description: We often hear that a classical education is one that cultivates wisdom and virtue, that nourishes the soul, and that seeks the Good, the True, and the Beautiful. These words are rich in meaning and worth spending some time contemplating. Join Molly Rychener for an interactive hour exploring the underlying themes of what makes a classical education a beautiful thing!

title: The Beauty of Learning
description: “Too often we have not been educating our humanity. We have been educating ourselves for doing rather than for being”. These words from Stratford Caldecott ought to resonate deeply with us when considering our utility-driven education system. It is important to consider the higher goals of education: tradition, wisdom, and virtue.Join Molly Rychener for a time of exploring the modes of teaching and learning and their place in guiding our students to those higher goals, and the beauty found throughout.

title: The Beauty of The Tapestry
description: The art of grammar develops a sensitivity to language; this language sensitivity is formed in reading, hearing, speaking, and writing the language. By attentively reading harmonious language, a student further becomes sensitive to harmonizing his or her own mind. By internalizing grammar’s form and symbols, which increases a sensitivity to language, one’s mind is liberated from discordant or confusing thoughts. Teachers can walk a few paths to develop language sensitivity in our students. Dictation, hearing and writing the language, develops attention, memory, imitation, and sensitivity. Narration, an act of remembering and retelling, is one path that develops language sensitivity. Join Molly Rychener in this active workshop during which we will practice dictation and narration (bring a pen and paper) before contemplating how to bring dictation into our classrooms.

title: The Conspiracy Against Boys: Why Boys Aren't Reading and What We can do About It
description: Most commentators want to say that boys have an aversion to books. But the problem is quite the opposite: books—modern books, that is, have an aversion to boys. A large part of the problem is that our schools, in alliance with publishing companies, have abandoned the books do not hide the cost of doing the right thing. We will discuss twelve great boys books, why boys need them, and where they can be found.

title: The Cultural War On Health - Making Families Healthy Again
description: The battle for our families’ health isn’t medical — it’s cultural. The Cultural War on Health exposes how modern systems have replaced stewardship with dependency, leaving families disconnected from the way they were designed to live. Dr. Dusty helps parents navigate the health care maze, rebuild confidence in their own bodies, and reclaim autonomy over their family’s well-being. Through simple, creation-based rhythms of food, movement, rest, and environment, this talk shows how to break free from confusion and return to the freedom of living by design.

title: The Differences Between Raising Sons and Daughters
The Encephalon Code: Remember EVERYTHING!
description: Many available curricula teach students what to learn, but not HOW to learn. Rote memorization, lack of focus, and general disinterest bring stress and anxiety to the homeschool experience. Using the latest Christ-centered neuroscience research, this fun, interactive session will introduce proven techniques for helping every student to: Focus better and retain more information; Memorize hundreds of facts easily; Read faster with full comprehension; Master new languages in a fraction of the usual time; Improve grades and discover a brighter future. These scientifically proven techniques work and the results are immediate! Come join us, parents and students. It’s time to unleash your superbrain.

title: THE FOUNDATIONAL FIVE: Bible Stories, Fairy Tales, Mother Goose, Aesop's Fables, and Mythology
description: David and his five smooth stones, Cinderella and her glass slipper, Mary and her lamb, the tortoise and the hare, and dynamite are only a few examples of how these five collections of tales have influenced our literature, our language, and our lives. Learning the prime stories gathered in these sources equips your students to enjoy a lifetime of reading and getting the most out of the allusions, metaphors, and subtle remarks mined from the great classics.

title: The Gospel Through the Eyes of Animals
description: Entertainment shapes the values of every generation. While Hollywood captures children’s imagination, the timeless truths of the Bible risk being forgotten. Kendu Kids, founded by former Disney animator Davy Liu (The Lion King, Beauty and the Beast), is reimagining classic Sunday school stories with creativity and wonder. Our mission is to build a strong biblical foundation for children and ignite their faith for generations to come.

title: The Homeschool Entrepreneur
description: Part 1 - Learn the basics and many benefits of starting a business with your homeschool family from the perspective of an entrepreneurial father and coach. Part 2 - Hear the perspective of a homeschooled teenager who has opened and managed multiple micro-businesses. Explore the benefits of learning by operating a business rather than taking a business class.

title: The Inciting Incident is Just the Beginning: Living Out Your Calling
description:
Think your faith story isn’t dramatic enough?

Join author CJ Milacci to discover how God is crafting an extraordinary purpose through your life right now.

Through the lens of storytelling, learn:

* The incredible power of a faithful testimony

* How today’s choices become tomorrow’s turning points

* The powerful ways God redeems our struggles

* How your unique gifts fit His master plan

* Ways to live intentionally for Christ right now

* Tools for living with eternal perspective

Perfect for parents and teens seeking to understand their role in God’s greater story and live purposefully for Him.

title: The Michael Clay Thompson Approach to English Language Arts Mastery
description: For more than 40 years Michael Clay Thompson has been developing an unique approach to teaching language arts in ways that children grasp quickly and enthusiastically and remember fondly forever after. His methods—based on the classics—will make your child the most outstanding student in university classes.

title: The Middle School Blueprint: How to Build the Brain, Confidence and Future Your Teen Needs Now
description: Middle school is more than a holding pattern—it’s a powerful launchpad where your child’s brain is pruned, passions emerge, and habits take root. In this energizing and practical session, Lisa Nehring shares how to use these years to prepare your tween for high school, college, and life. Learn what academics matter most, how to introduce early college classes and certifications, and how to create a connected, real-world homeschool that builds curiosity, confidence, and character. You’ll leave with a clear plan to help your student grow in identity, independence, and purpose.

title: The Miraculous History of America: God's Hand on Us!
description: Understanding God’s role in the foundation of America is vitally important to maintaining freedom. It’s something our founders knew and relied on, and is often the missing ingredient in our modern-day fight for liberty. This message is not only enlightening but incredibly empowering.

title: The Modern Parent's Guide to Raising Resilient Kids
description: Join this workshop to unpack the surprising truth about resiliency and why our kids don’t need to “toughen up” — they need to feel safe, connected, and understood. You’ll walk away with a simple, modern framework for navigating big feelings, shutdowns, and everyday challenges with calm leadership your child can rely on.

title: The Other College Track: Trades, Tech, and the Path to Prosperity
description: While everyone’s chasing college admissions, the smart money is on skills. Electricians, welders, and technicians may be the next millionaires—and your homeschooler can be one of them. In this inspiring, practical session, Lisa Nehring explores the booming world of trades and tech, highlighting alternative routes like industry certifications, apprenticeships, and associate degree programs that lead to meaningful, debt-free careers. Discover how to help your teen build confidence, competence, and a future that truly works.

title: The Power of Words: Seven Phrases That Nurture Hearts and Connection
The Right to LIFE: States Rights Issue or Nah?
description: The Supreme Court ruled that abortion is a states right issue. First, is this true according to the constitution? Second, with so many states “legalizing” abortion, how can we combat it to save the lives of the innocent? Abortion is still a topic that generates animus for so many and we need to continue to bring truth to the confusion and hope to our nation.

title: The Storybook Family Rhythm
description: A simple family tradition has transformed our family; we read aloud together morning and night. Learn how to implement this imperfect yet powerful habit to grow a stronger family bond, travel the world without leaving home, and build the imagination and education of your children for a lifetime.

title: The Stronger Boy Way
description: Establishing rites of passage for a boy's journey into strong and healthy masculinity.

title: The Student Becomes the Teacher: Turning Test Anxiety into Confidence
description: Workshop Description #1: Test anxiety affects nearly every student at some point, often leaving them overwhelmed, unprepared, and doubting themselves. But anxiety can be reframed—not as a barrier, but as usable energy for focus, confidence, and performance. In this interactive workshop, Dennis DiNoia, M.A.Ed. (“Mr. D”), draws from his book to share practical strategies that turn test anxiety into confidence by combining research-backed tools with real-world teaching techniques.

title: The Teenage Brain Unlocked: A Neuroscience Guide to HS'ing Adolescents
description: The Teenage Brain Unlocked: A Neuroscience Guide to Homeschooling in the Adolescent Years Teaching a teen or pre-teen at home can feel like navigating a whole new world—because it is! During adolescence, the brain undergoes a massive remodel that affects memory, motivation, emotional regulation, and decision-making. In this workshop, Kathryn Gomes, M.Ed., will guide you through the research on how teens think, learn, and process information, and what that means for your homeschool. Being informed helps us live with our kids in an understanding way and grasping the way they are wired lets us homeschool more effectively. With a mix of neuroscience and practical tips, this session will help you approach your teen’s education with empathy, insight, and effectiveness.

title: The Three Publishing Paths
description:
How does a manuscript actually become a book? How do authors get paid? How do I know I'm not being taken advantage of?
The world of publishing is rife with scams. However, there are still many great opportunities for authors to publish books and get paid. If we know what to look for, there are more ways to successfully publish today than ever before.
In easy-to-understand, straightforward language, bestelling author and writing coach Brad Pauquette breaks down the main types of opportunities that authors will find in today's publishing ecosystem, and the common scams and pitfalls to avoid.

title: The Top 10 Myths Related to Choosing the Right College
description: We live, work and learn in an intricate, dynamic, and complex world. Today’s high school students and their families have a remarkable opportunity to wholeheartedly embrace God’s calling, strive for excellence in their chosen careers, foster meaningful relationships through active engagement and service, and play a crucial role in creating a better future. To successfully navigate the joy and complexity of the college process, it is imperative that parents are equipped to ask the right questions and develop a more comprehensive understanding of the consequences of a student’s college decision. Learn more about the Top 10 Myths of the college evaluation and selection journey.

title: The Two Pathways to Calm: Transforming Reactive Storms Into Chosen Peace
description: Why do some moments feel like emotional explosions waiting to happen, no matter how hard we try? In this eye-opening class, Nicholeen Peck reveals the two distinct internal pathways every parent and child takes when stress hits: the Reactive Storm Pathway and the Chosen Calmness Pathway. Drawing from her new book The Power of Calm, as well as years of working with strong-willed and neurodiverse youth in her treatment foster home, Nicholeen will show parents how we can teach ourselves and our children to interrupt the storm before it starts. Learn simple, proven, self-government skills that activate the prefrontal cortex and create real, steady calm even on the toughest days.

title: The Ultimate 10-Step College Admissions Strategy
description: Join HelloCollege founder Kevin Krebs for a step-by-step guide to navigating the college admissions process for homeschool families. Learn how to map milestones, build a balanced college list, and craft standout essays, all while keeping your family aligned and on track. Discover proven strategies to stay organized, meet key deadlines, and position your family for college admission success.

title: The Very Best, Hands-On, Kinda Dangerous Family Devotions
description: Teaching our kids about God and the principles he’s given us to live by…we know we need to do this—but how do we do it in a way where kids will listen—even teens? Tim will demonstrate practical, powerful object lessons in this “how-to” and “hands-on” session so that you leave equipped to do the job at home—even if you’ve tried and failed before. Simple things like a microwave, leaf blower, toaster, and more will be used to teach lessons kids will never forget!

title: Three Essential Promises to Consider When Selecting a College
description: Selecting the right college is one of the most critical decisions a student will ever face. This choice is not just about education—it's a crucial step toward future opportunities. With a vast array of college options, families must invest their time and resources in thoroughly evaluating these possibilities. Discover the “Bermuda Triangle of Higher Education” and understand the three essential promises that can guide students in choosing a college that not only complements but also reinforces the educational foundation laid by their families. This is more than a decision; it's the key to a successful future.

title: Tips and Tricks for Adapting Curriculum for Struggling Learners
