description: Homeschooling can seem so simple. Buy a whiteboard and dry-erase markers, hang a wall map and some posters in your dining room, and go to work. But then real life intervenes. Your wiggler and your giggler can't seem to settle; books and worksheets send your struggling reader into orbit; your otherwise complacent children raise the flag of revolt; and you find yourself face-planted before the Lord. Join Julia Nalle, mother to three adopted and two biological kids, co-writer of BiblioPlan and teacher for 35-plus years, as she facilitates an honest and heartfelt discussion for parents who are scratching their heads over their struggling or special needs learners. In this session she will discuss some of the reasons why your child may be struggling and share practical, fun strategies for helping your child.

title: Twelve Great Books for Boys
description: Two Russians, two Englishmen, two Frenchmen, three Americans, a South African, and a Norwegian walk into a bar... Not really. But they do come together in this list of the greatest Christian literary works of the last two centuries. It is a list that includes two Learn why these books are great and how it can be that, as someone once said, the worst Christians write the best Christian books. Learn which are the best translations and why it is okay to listen, rather than actually read them (along with who are the best readers).

title: Unboxing the World: Bring Culture and Cuisine to Your Homeschool
description: Discover how Eat2Explore can transform your homeschool into a global adventure! In this interactive session, Homeschool Representative Lisa Jaeger will show you how to use Eat2Explore’s hands-on cooking kits and cultural studies in a variety of ways — from short unit studies to full-year geography, history, and cultural programs. You’ll explore practical ways to weave in language arts, science, and even math connections, all while cooking and learning together as a family. Whether you’re looking for a one-week taste of another country or a year-long world tour, you’ll leave with creative ideas, ready-to-use resources, and a fresh excitement for learning at home.

title: Unlock the Secrets of the Modern Pianist: Composing Made Simple For You
description: Parents once paid for "traditional" lessons—scales, sight reading, and an occasional recital. But today, they want more. Students crave real-world skills: arranging songs they love, composing original music, and recording tracks that sound professional. Most teachers can’t offer this—which means if you can, you instantly set yourself apart. With our framework, you'll stay relevant, inspire creativity, and become the teacher parents seek out first.

title: Unlocking Your Child's Genius: A Fun Dive into Learning Styles
description: Every child learns differently—some are hands-on adventurers, others absorb knowledge through stories, and a few might just be mini mad scientists! This upbeat workshop is your guide to unlocking your child’s genius by understanding their unique learning style. Through laughter, interactive activities, and plenty of “aha!” moments, we’ll explore how to tailor learning to your child’s strengths. Bonus: we’ll even compare learning styles to your morning coffee orders—because education should be as energizing as your caffeine fix!

title: Unschooling: A Fresh Take on Learning
description: Discover the philosophy and practice of unschooling with Kate. She’ll share insights into how self-directed learning can unlock a child’s natural curiosity and build lifelong skills. You’ll walk away with fresh ideas for embracing a more flexible, interest-led approach to education.

title: Using Algebra Tiles for Hands on Math for Grades 6-12
description:
This workshop is designed for parents who want to enhance their teaching of algebraic concepts to students in grades 6-12 using algebra tiles. Algebra tiles are powerful, hands-on manipulatives that make abstract ideas more concrete, providing a visual and tactile way to explore mathematical operations and concepts.
Participants will:
• Learn the basics of algebra tiles, including the different types of tiles and how they represent variables and constants.
• Learn how to use algebra tiles for basic integer operations including adding, subtracting, multiplying and dividing
• Explore how to use algebra tiles to model and solve linear equations, simplify expressions, factor polynomials, and perform operations such as addition, subtraction, and multiplication of algebraic expressions.
• Be provided multiple resources for using algebra tiles both free and paid including a handout to create your own, as well as the information to download a free virtual set of algebra tiles and where you can purchase a 3-d plastic set of algebra tiles

By the end of the workshop, parents will have the confidence and tools to integrate algebra tiles into their math curriculum, making concepts more accessible and enjoyable for all students.

title: Using Literature as a Springboard for Interdisciplinary Units
description:
Workshop Objectives
This workshop is designed to help participants discover effective strategies for engaging students in learning by utilizing literature as the foundation for interdisciplinary instruction. Through a focus on historical literature, attendees will explore methods for teaching social studies, English Language Arts, constitutional principles, ethics, and character education or life skills in a unified approach. The workshop will emphasize aligning curriculum with educational goals and standards to ensure comprehensive learning outcomes.

Workshop Format and Activities
The session begins by examining the reasons for and approaches to selecting literature for classroom use. Participants will learn how to teach and co-teach units, as well as how to design and pace instructional units effectively. A key feature of the workshop includes a practical example: a unit on World War II centered around the narrative memoir, “A Young Man of the Front Line: Lessons of War.” This unit is accompanied by integrated lesson plans, providing concrete illustrations of interdisciplinary teaching techniques.
Skills addressed during the workshop include integrating multiple subjects, developing lesson plans and pacing guides, and planning integration activities. Participants will also learn strategies for collaborating with fellow teachers to create cross-curricular projects. Sample assignments will demonstrate ways to connect interactive student or team projects to contemporary issues and real-world skills, enhancing the relevance of classroom learning.

title: Visual English: Sentence Diagramming for the Bold and Daring
description: Understanding your first language is a prerequisite for learning another language, or becoming a good communicator. In a world where language skills are in decline and most students rely on AI to correct their grammar, the ability to communicate with clarity has become a valuable commodity. Do you want your student to boldly go where no language student has gone before? Are you daring in your approach to languages, especially when learning new languages? Sentence diagramming is the best and most powerful tool that can demystify English.

title: Ways to Pay for College
description: Exploring the different avenues of paying for college.

title: We Must Raise Honorable Ladies & Gentlemen!
description: Of course we want to raise honorable, kind humans who are able to show others that they are valued but parents, this requires very purposeful parenting. I guarantee that your mind and heart will be transformed in this workshop and you will have a new perspective on how important these simple lessons for your children are. Really, don't miss this! You will learn how to raise true ladies and gentlemen.

title: What is a Hymnal (and Why We Need Them Back in the Pews)
What is an American: the 250 Year Old Question
description: With so many competing ideologies—socialism, communism, Marxism—and a variety of religious perspectives, it is more important than ever to define what it means to be an American. America has a unique way of life that has endured for 250 years, attracting people from around the world for over two centuries. Understanding this way of life is essential to knowing what it truly means to be an American.

title: What is Education For? Why Trying to Prepare Your Student for a STEM Job is a Big Mistake
description: An increased focus on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) is now seen as the way out of the education crisis by many educational policymakers. It is only the most recent manifestation of the stress on vocationalism in our schools. But is the narrowing of the focus of our curricula really the answer to our educational woes? And is the abandonment of a focus on the arts and humanities the best way to accomplish even the goals of the STEM movement? Learn why classical education's focus on the liberal arts and the traditional humanities not only makes for better human beings, but provides the best foundation for any career. And learn how best to articulate this vision to your parents and your community.

title: What to Say and How to Say it to Your Teen--5 Tricky Conversations
description: These are critical years- and how we handle tricky conversations make all the difference in how these homeschooling years end. We'll talk about how to prep, what not to say, handling objections, and more. Here's five tricky conversations we'll give insights on.

title: When Emotions Take Over: Emotional Regulation Strategies for Homeschool Days
description: Homeschooling brings unique joys—but it can also bring big emotions. From frustration over schoolwork to sensory overload and sibling conflict, emotional outbursts can quickly derail the day. This session explores strategies for co-regulation, building in sensory breaks, and connecting emotional regulation to executive functioning skills. Parents will learn concrete techniques to defuse meltdowns before they escalate, while fostering an environment where children feel safe, understood, and supported. Whether your child is neurodivergent or simply struggles with self-control, you’ll leave with actionable ideas to bring more peace, stability, and success to your homeschool days.

title: Where Learning Clicks: A Personalized Learning Experience for Every Child
description: With over 22 years of experience in education, Dr. Tamra Lanning is the Director of Customer Success at Learning Hub, an innovative company supporting homeschool families and K–12 students through engaging, adaptive learning experiences. A former educator and school leader, Tamra is passionate about empowering parents to personalize learning and help every child grow at their own pace. At Learning Hub, she leads initiatives that make quality instruction and meaningful progress accessible to all learners.

title: Why Is Academic Writing Difficult and How Do We Make It Easier for Kids?
description:
For many students, academic writing is a continual source of frustration and resentment. The way we make writing easier for kids is to show them where their ideas go on the page. This helps them organize their great ideas and structure their writing. In fact, learning how to get their ideas from their heads onto the page in an organized way is the single most important writing skill students need to learn.

In this workshop, you’ll learn a revolutionary writing method that uses colors to show students how to structure their writing. You can then use this easy-to-follow method the very next day to help your child become a confident and successful academic writer.

title: Why Latin? The Core of a Classical Education
description: Many are attracted to classical education but are puzzled by the centrality of Latin. Can’t we switch to Spanish? Why not Greek or Hebrew? This talk will explain the indispensable role of Latin in a classical curriculum. (Hint: It’s about way more than just learning to read Latin.)

title: Why Leave Schooling to Do More Schooling?
description: Education and discipleship are not the same as mass schooling. They have different traditions and different expectations. Have you ever asked, “How do I know if they are on pace?”, “What if they miss something?”, “How do I know if they are learning?”, or even “I’m not sure I know enough to teach high school!” then this session should settle your mind and help you plan for discipleship with excitement. Explore some of the key differences and save massive amounts of time and stress trying to recreate schooling at home.

title: Why Life Skills Belong at the Center of Your Homeschool
description: In a culture where many young adults lack confidence and practical competence, homeschool families have a unique opportunity to raise capable, hireable adults through intentional life skills training. This session makes the case that skills like gardening, cooking, food preservation, and animal care aren't just extras; in fact, they're core education that builds discipline, problem-solving, and the ability to adapt in unfamiliar situations. Learn a practical framework for helping kids move from helping to true ownership without constant reminders, and gain confidence that life skills actually strengthen academic success rather than compete with it. Walk away with a renewed vision for raising young adults who are dependable, adaptable, and prepared to contribute and lead in college, career, home, and family life. Students are welcome to attend this session

title: Why Won’t They Listen? The Obedience Blueprint Every Homeschool Needs
description: A peaceful, productive homeschool begins with one foundational skill: obedience. Yet many parents feel stuck in daily battles—nagging, reminding, negotiating, pushback, or feeling ignored—despite doing everything they know how to do. The problem isn’t the parent or the child. The problem is missing tools. In this life-giving class, Nicholeen Peck teaches the essential skills that help children, youth, and parents communicate clearly, strengthen relationships, and solve problems with calmness and confidence. She’ll share a Self-Government Toolkit built on decades of experience with strong-willed and neurodiverse children and youth. Families don’t thrive by accident—they thrive through unity and skill development.

title: Wielding Your Media Parental Rights with Strength and Wisdom
description: When it comes to media, the forces of the world are using every possible avenue to allure and capture your child’s heart. Videos, books, movies, and games are produced with the intent to persuade your child away from your family’s values. And these forces are powerful. How can parents learn to wield their rights with strength and wisdom to protect their kids? In this workshop, publisher and teacher Kiri Jorgensen will educate you on the current challenging media trends, give you actionable tools to build the structure and boundaries your kids need, and help you understand the power you also hold.

title: Will They Be Ready? How Life Skills Shape Confident, Capable Young Adults
Word Problems: From Flustered to Fearless
description: Practical strategies that replace tears with triumphs — helping students actually enjoy solving word problems!

title: Work at Home as a Medical Transcriptionist
description: There’s a huge demand for good medical transcriptionists. It’s a great job for teens or adults, part- or full-time. Discover a step-by-step guide to what medical transcription is, medical transcription versus medical billing & coding, earning potential, equipment needed, the reports we transcribe, and more.

title: Write a Novel - Change the World
description: A powerful story possesses the unique ability to weave its way into the collective soul of society. By crafting relatable characters, compelling plots, and vibrant settings, authors influence people’s views about themselves and the world they live in. Hollywood knows this is true and so they're spending billions of dollars to send their messages across the globe. It’s time to release the power of story as a force for purpose and earn English Language Arts credit at the same time! In this workshop, author and educator, Sarah Reid, will show parents and students the first step towards wielding a pen and winning hearts—how to write a novel that will change the world.

title: Write Powerful Fiction...Secrets to Showing Your Story and Using Deep Point of View
description: “DEEP Point-of-View” and “Show don’t tell” are two critically important areas of fiction. Get it right and you’ve got killer fiction. Mess it up and your fiction is already dead. We’ll show you how to make your story come alive.

title: You Can Homeschool Your Neurodivergent Learner—and We Can Help!
description: Unpack the frequently asked questions of how to homeschool struggling learners and those who learn differently. Be encouraged and equipped as Cheri Kay shares diagnostic checklists for struggling learners, discusses common roadblocks homeschooling parents face (and how to navigate around them), and gives strategies for achieving success with your children who learn differently.
